parameters.description: UUID of the target entity (customer, organization, or link). For customer relationships, this is optional for backward compatibility.

post.description:
"Creates a new plugin integration. As part of creation, Productboard sends a
**probe request** (GET) to your configured action URL to verify it is reachable
and intends to receive action notifications. See the `callbacks` section below.

The integration is created in the `enabled` state by default. Set
`fields.integrationStatus` to `disabled` to skip the probe (useful for staged setup).

**`action` is write-only**: the action configuration, including any
`headers.authorization` secret, is never returned in responses.
"

get.description:
Sent during integration creation (and on PATCH when `enabled` and `action.url`
changes) to verify your endpoint is reachable and intends to receive action
notifications.

- Response must be `200 OK` with `Content-Type: text/plain`
- Response body must echo the `validationToken` query parameter **exactly**
- Timeout: **5 seconds**
- TLS certificate must be valid and signed by a public authority
- Failure immediately fails the create/update request with `callback.probeFailed`

This request is sent with `Accept: text/plain`.

**Security**: set `data.fields.action.headers.authorization` to have
Productboard include it in every outgoing request to your endpoint.

parameters.name: validationToken
parameters.description: Token to echo verbatim in the response body.

200.description: Echo the `validationToken` value as plain text.

post.description:
Sent when a user clicks a plugin integration button in the Productboard UI.

**Trigger types** (from `data.trigger`):
- `button.push` — user clicked to connect / push the entity
- `button.unlink` — user clicked to unlink the connected entity
- `button.dismiss` — user dismissed an error popup

Respond with a `ConnectionState` to indicate the outcome. This is equivalent
to calling the Configure Connection endpoint with the same state.

**Recommended behavior:**
- `button.push` → respond with `connected`, or `progress` for async processing
- `button.unlink` → respond with `initial` to reset the connection
- `button.dismiss` → respond with `initial` to clear the error

**Timeout: 5 seconds.** For async processing, respond with `progress`
immediately and update the connection later via the Configure Connection
endpoint (`data.links.connection` in the payload).

**Security**: set `data.fields.action.headers.authorization` to authenticate
incoming requests from Productboard.

200.description:
Respond with the resulting connection state. Any valid `ConnectionState`
is accepted. Setting `state: initial` resets the connection (equivalent
to deleting it).

get.description:
"Returns all plugin integrations for the workspace.

Paginated using cursor-based pagination. Follow `links.next` to fetch the next page.
When `links.next` is `null`, you have reached the last page.

**OAuth2 isolation**: OAuth2 access tokens only see integrations created by the same
OAuth2 application. Public API access tokens see all workspace integrations.
"

"Returns a single plugin integration by ID.

**OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the integration was created by a different
OAuth2 application or by a public API access token (existence is masked for security).
"

patch.description:
"Partially updates a plugin integration. Fields not included in the request preserve
their current value.

If the integration is `enabled` and `fields.action.url` is updated, Productboard
sends a new probe to verify the updated endpoint. No probe is sent when `disabled`.

**`action` is write-only**: never returned in responses.
"

delete.description:
"Permanently deletes a plugin integration and all its associated connections.
Entities that previously had connections to this integration will have their
push buttons removed. This action cannot be undone.

**OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the integration was created by a different
OAuth2 application or by a public API access token.
"

get.description:
Returns all connections for a plugin integration, **excluding** those in the
`initial` state. The `initial` state is the default — it means no connection has
been established for that entity yet.

Paginated using cursor-based pagination. Follow `links.next` to fetch the next page.
When `links.next` is `null`, you have reached the last page.

"Returns the connection for a specific entity within a plugin integration.

**Important**: if no connection exists for the entity, this endpoint returns
a connection with `state: initial` rather than a `404`. The `initial` state
indicates the entity has not yet been pushed to the third-party system.
"

put.description:
"Creates or replaces the connection state for a specific entity within a plugin
integration. This is an **upsert** — if no connection exists it is created; if one
exists it is replaced.

**Connection states:**

| State | Effect |
|---|---|
| `connected` | Entity is linked; push button shows connection details |
| `error` | Connection failed; push button shows error state |
| `progress` | Processing asynchronously; push button shows loading indicator |
| `initial` | Equivalent to deleting the connection; push button resets |

**Async processing flow:**
1. User clicks push button → your endpoint receives an action notification
2. Respond immediately with `state: progress` to show a loading indicator
3. Process the action asynchronously
4. Call this endpoint with the final state (`connected` or `error`)
"

delete.description:
"Permanently deletes the connection between a entity and the third-party system,
resetting the push button to its initial unconnected state.

This is equivalent to calling the Configure Connection endpoint with `state: initial`.
"

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of teams from your workspace.
Teams represent groups of members that can be used for organizing work and assigning ownership.

- Teams are sorted by creation date, newest first.
- Use the `name` parameter to filter by name (case-insensitive exact match).
- Use the `handle` parameter to filter by handle (case-insensitive exact match).
- Use the `query` parameter to search by partial name or handle (case-insensitive). For example, `query=product` will match "Product Team", "My Product", "production", etc.
- Use the `pageCursor` parameter to paginate through results.

parameters.name: pageCursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter teams by name (case-insensitive exact match).

parameters.name: handle
parameters.description: Filter teams by handle (case-insensitive exact match).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Full-text search query. Performs case-insensitive partial match on team name and handle.

post.description:
"Creates a new team in your workspace.
You must provide the team `name` and a unique `handle` for @mentions.

## Required Fields

- `name`: The name of the team (1-255 characters, must be unique per workspace)
- `handle`: Unique handle for @mentions (lowercase alphanumeric only, 1-255 characters, must be unique per workspace)

## Response

Returns a reference to the created team with its ID and self link.
Use the GET endpoint to retrieve the full team details.
"

"## Purpose
Performs a filtered search over teams using a POST request.
Use this endpoint for batch lookups by IDs, names, or handles when query string filters are insufficient.

## Request Format

Supports two formats. Format is auto-detected by the presence of `filter` or `search` keys.

**New format** (recommended): Uses structured `filter` and `search` objects.
**Legacy format** (deprecated): Uses flat arrays directly under `data`.

## Key Features
- `filter.id`: Filter by team UUIDs (OR logic, max 100)
- `filter.fields.name`: Filter by team name, case-insensitive (single string or array, OR logic)
- `filter.fields.handle`: Filter by team handle, case-insensitive (single string or array, OR logic)
- `search.query`: Full-text search on team name and handle (case-insensitive partial match)
- Returns the same response format as `GET /v2/teams`

## Filter Logic
- Multiple values within a single filter use **OR** logic (e.g., `filter.fields.name: [\"A\", \"B\"]` returns teams named A or B)
- Different filter types and search use **AND** logic (e.g., `filter` AND `search` = intersection)
- Empty request returns all teams (same as `GET /v2/teams`)

## Important Notes
- Each filter array accepts at most 100 items; exceeding this returns a 400 error
- Pagination uses cursor-based navigation via `pageCursor` query parameter
"

parameters.name: pageCursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination.

filter_by_fields.summary: Filter by name and handle (new format)

get.description:
Retrieves the full details of a specific team by its unique identifier (UUID).
Returns the team's name, handle, description, avatar URL, and metadata such as creation and update timestamps.

Use this endpoint to load complete data for a single team.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Team identifier.

patch.description:
"Updates the fields and/or members of an existing team.
Supports both field updates via `fields` and member management via `patch` operations.
Both can be combined in a single request.

## Updatable Fields

- `name`: The name of the team (1-255 characters)
- `handle`: Unique handle for @mentions (lowercase alphanumeric only)
- `description`: Optional description of the team (max 10,000 characters)

## Patch Operations on Members

Use `patch` array to manage team members with these operations:

- `addItems`: Add members to the team (idempotent, skips already-present members)
- `removeItems`: Remove members from the team (idempotent, skips already-absent members)
- `set`: Replace entire member list atomically
- `clear`: Remove all members from the team

Maximum 100 member references per operation.

## Response

Returns a reference to the updated team.
Use the GET endpoint to retrieve the full updated team details.
"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Team identifier.

Permanently deletes a team from your workspace.
This operation removes the team and all its membership associations.

**Use this endpoint with caution — deletion is irreversible.**

post.description:
"Creates a new webhook subscription to be actively notified on each change in the specified entities.

As part of subscription creation, Productboard validates the notification URL:
it must use `https`, have a publicly resolvable host, and not point to localhost,
loopback, site-local, or internal addresses.

**Scope requirements**: the OAuth2 application must have the scopes required by all requested event types.
"

Sent whenever a subscribed entity change event occurs.

- Timeout: **5 seconds** (end-to-end)
- Return any `2XX` status to acknowledge receipt
- Prefer `204 No Content` — response body is ignored
- Failed deliveries are retried with exponential backoff (up to 8 attempts: 1m, 3m, 9m, ...)
- Process notifications asynchronously and return immediately to avoid timeouts
- Too many timeouts or failures may result in Productboard **disabling the subscription** with notification loss

**Security**: configure `data.fields.notification.headers.authorization` to authenticate
incoming requests from Productboard.

200.description: Any `2XX` status is treated as a successful delivery. `204 No Content` is preferred.

get.description:
"Returns all webhook subscriptions for the workspace. Returns up to 100 items per page by default.

Paginated using cursor-based pagination. Follow `links.next` in the response to fetch the next page.
When `links.next` is `null`, you have reached the last page.

**OAuth2 isolation**: OAuth2 access tokens only see subscriptions created by the same OAuth2 application.
Public API access tokens see all workspace subscriptions.
"

200.description: A paginated list of webhook subscriptions

get.description:
"Returns a single webhook subscription by ID.

**OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the subscription was created by a different OAuth2 application
or by a public API access token (existence is masked for security).
"

delete.description:
"Permanently deletes a webhook subscription. No further notifications will be sent after deletion. **OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the subscription was created by a different OAuth2 application
or by a public API access token.
"

get.description:
Returns configuration metadata for note types, including supported fields, patch operations, and validation rules.

Use this endpoint to programmatically discover which fields are available, what note types are supported,
and how your workspace is configured.

### Filtering by Type

By default, this endpoint returns configurations for all available note types (`textNote`, `conversationNote`, `opportunityNote`).

You can filter results to specific types using the optional `type[]` query parameter:

- `type[]=textNote`
- `type[]=textNote&type[]=conversationNote`

For backward compatibility, the API also accepts a single `type` query parameter with one value,
for example `type=textNote`.

Legacy aliases (`simple`, `conversation`, `opportunity`) are also accepted and normalized to the canonical values above.

parameters.name: type[]
parameters.description:
"Optional array of note types to filter results.
Valid values: textNote, conversationNote, opportunityNote.
Legacy aliases simple, conversation, opportunity are also accepted.
Example: type[]=textNote&type[]=conversationNote
"

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
"Optional single note type filter for backward compatibility.
Valid values: textNote, conversationNote, opportunityNote.
Legacy aliases simple, conversation, opportunity are also accepted.
Example: type=textNote
Note: Use `type[]` to filter by multiple note types.
"

get.description:
Returns configuration metadata for a specific note type, including supported fields, patch operations, and validation rules.

Use this endpoint to programmatically discover the configuration for a specific note type.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The note type to retrieve configuration for. Legacy note type aliases are also accepted.

post.description:
Creates a new note in your workspace.
Notes represent individual pieces of feedback from customers, internal teams, or external systems.

You must specify the `type` of note you're creating:
- `"textNote"` - for plain, unstructured notes
- `"conversationNote"` - for structured messages (e.g., from chat, email, or support systems)

Legacy aliases (`simple`, `conversation`, `opportunity`) are accepted on input and normalized before business rules are applied.
> ⚠️ Notes of type `opportunityNote` cannot be created via the API.

## Field Requirements and Configuration

The `fields.name` attribute is **required** for all note types.
Other fields depend on the selected note type and your workspace configuration.
Use the [`/v2/notes/configurations`](#/paths/~1v2~1notes~1configurations/get) endpoint to discover available fields for each type.

Notes can also be linked to Customers entities or Product links (like features) via relationships.

get.description:
"Retrieves a list of notes from your workspace.

> Without the `members:pii:read` scope, owner and creator email fields are returned as `[redacted]`. Filtering by `owner[email]` or `creator[email]` requires the `members:pii:read` scope.

- Notes are sorted by creation date, newest first.
- You can filter results using query parameters like `archived`, `processed`, `owner[email]`, `creator[email]`, `source[recordId]`, or date/time ranges.
- Use the `pageCursor` parameter to paginate through results.
- Use the `fields` query parameter to optimize response size (default: all non-null fields, use `fields=all` to include null values, or `fields=name,tags` for specific fields).

> To discover available fields
use the [`/v2/notes/configurations`](#/paths/~1v2~1notes~1configurations/get) endpoint.

### Date/Time Filtering

You can filter notes by creation or update timestamps using ISO-8601 date-time format:
- Use `createdFrom` and `createdTo` to filter by creation date (inclusive bounds)
- Use `updatedFrom` and `updatedTo` to filter by update date (inclusive bounds)
- All date/time filters can be combined with each other and with other filters
- Date ranges must be valid (From <= To) or a 400 error will be returned

### Filtering combinations

Some combinations of the `archived` and `processed` flags result in empty or unexpected results.
Use the following table to understand which notes are returned based on the filter values:

| `archived` | `processed` | Result                    |
|------------|-------------|---------------------------|
| –          | –           | All notes                 |
| `true`     | `true`      | None                      |
| `true`     | `false`     | Archived                  |
| `false`    | `true`      | Processed                 |
| `false`    | `false`     | Unprocessed               |
| `true`     | –           | Archived                  |
| `false`    | –           | Processed + unprocessed   |
| –          | `true`      | Processed                 |
| –          | `false`     | Archived + unprocessed    |

> **Note**: Archived notes always return `processed: false` in the API response, regardless of their actual processing state. This is a known limitation of the system.
"

parameters.description:
Filter notes created on or before this date/time (inclusive).
Must be in ISO-8601 format (e.g., "2023-12-31T23:59:59Z").
Can be combined with createdFrom to define a range.

Filter notes updated on or after this date/time (inclusive).
Must be in ISO-8601 format (e.g., "2023-10-01T12:00:00Z").

parameters.description:
Filter notes updated on or before this date/time (inclusive).

Can be combined with updatedFrom to define a range.

post.description:
Performs a filtered search over notes using a POST request.
This endpoint supports the same filtering capabilities as `GET /v2/notes`, but allows more complex filtering through relationships.

> Filtering by `owners.email` or `creators.email` requires the `members:pii:read` scope.

### Filter Logic

- Multiple values within a relationship filter use **OR** logic (e.g., customer with id1 OR id2)
- Different filter types use **AND** logic (e.g., customer AND link filters)

### Relationships

Filter notes by their relationships to customers (users or companies) and links (features):

- `customer.ids` - Filter by customer or company UUIDs (OR logic within)
- `link.ids` - Filter by linked feature UUIDs (OR logic within)

parameters.name: pageCursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination.

parameters.name: fields[]
parameters.description:
"**Deprecated**: Use `return.fields` in the request body instead.

Controls which fields are returned in the response.
"

get.description:
Retrieves the full details of a specific note by its ID.

> Without the `members:pii:read` scope, owner and creator email fields are returned as `[redacted]`.

By default, the response includes all non-null fields available in your workspace for that note type
(e.g. `textNote`, `conversationNote`). You can optimize the response size by using the `fields` query parameter:
- Use `fields=name,tags,content` to retrieve only specific fields
- Use `fields=all` to include all fields, even those with null values

You can use this endpoint to display the entire note content, including metadata such as owner, tags, and relationships.

> Use the [`/v2/notes/configurations`](#/paths/~1v2~1notes~1configurations/get) endpoint
to discover which fields are available for your workspace.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note identifier.

fields.name: My note
fields.content: This is a sample note content.

patch.description:
"Updates the values of one or more fields for an existing note.

You can update fields using either:
- **Field Updates**: Replace entire field values using the `fields` object
- **Patch Operations**: Perform granular updates using the `patch` array with operations (`set`, `clear`, `addItems`, `removeItems`)

Use this endpoint to update note metadata such as name, owner, tags, or content.
Use the [`/v2/notes/configurations`](#/paths/~1v2~1notes~1configurations/get) endpoint to discover available fields and possible patch operations.

### Patch Operations

The following patch operations are supported:

| Operation | Description | Supported Fields |
|-----------|-------------|------------------|
| `set` | Set a field to a specific value | All fields |
| `clear` | Clear/reset a field to its default value | `owner`, `tags` |
| `addItems` | Add items to array fields | `tags`, `content` (conversationNote) |
| `removeItems` | Remove items from array fields | `tags`, `content` (conversationNote) |

**Field-specific patch operation support:**
- **owner**: `set`, `clear`
- **tags**: `set`, `clear`, `addItems`, `removeItems`
- **archived**: `set`
- **processed**: `set`
- **name**: `set`
- **content** (textNote): `set`
- **content** (conversationNote): `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`

### Known Limitations

**Content updates for notes with linked features**: Notes that have content linked to features (via highlights/snippets) cannot have their `content` field updated. Attempting to update the content will return a `422 Unprocessable Entity` error with code `validation.forbidden`. You can still update other fields such as `name`, `owner`, `tags`, `archived`, and `processed` on these notes.

**Archive and Processed Field Behavior**: When you set `archived` to `false`, the note will be set to processed automatically by the system. This is a known limitation where unarchiving a note triggers processing regardless of the previous processed state. The `processed` field will reflect the actual processing status after the unarchive operation.
"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note identifier.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes a note from your workspace. This action is irreversible and should be used with caution.

Once deleted, the note and all of its content, metadata, and relationships
will be removed and cannot be recovered.

> To perform a soft-delete (archiving), use an update call instead to set the `archived` flag.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note identifier.

get.description:
Retrieves all relationships connected to a specific note.

This includes:
- Customer relationship (either a User or Company entity)
- Product link relationships (e.g. linked features)

### Customer relationship rules
- A note can have a relationship to **exactly one customer entity** (User or Company).
- If the note has a **User** as the customer, and that User is associated with a Company,
  only the **User** is returned.
- If the note has a **User** without a Company, the User is returned.
- If the note has a **Company**, the Company is returned.
- If the note has no customer relationship, an empty array is returned.

### Product link relationships
- Notes can be linked to **multiple Product Management entities** (e.g. features, components).
- All such relationships are returned in the same response.

> To discover supported relationship types, use the [`/v2/notes/configurations`](#/paths/~1v2~1notes~1configurations/get) endpoint.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note identifier.

parameters.name: pageCursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination through relationships

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of relationships to return per page

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Filter relationships by type. When provided, only relationships of the specified type are returned.
If not provided, all relationship types are returned.

parameters.name: target[type]
parameters.description:
"Filter relationships by the target entity type. When provided, only relationships
where the target matches the specified entity type are returned.
If not provided, all target types are returned.

Available values:
- `user`: Customer relationships where the target is a user
- `company`: Customer relationships where the target is a company
- `feature`: Link relationships where the target is a feature
- `subfeature`: Link relationships where the target is a subfeature
- `product`: Link relationships where the target is a product
- `component`: Link relationships where the target is a component
"

post.description:
Creates a new relationship between a note and another entity.

You can link a note to:
- A Product Management entity (e.g. feature, component, initiative)
- A customer entity (company or user)

This is useful for capturing customer feedback and tying it to the right product link,
or for attributing a note to a specific customer.

> If a Customer relationship already exists (user or company), it will be replaced.
Notes can have **one customer** relationship and **multiple product link** relationships.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note identifier.

put.description:
Sets or replaces the customer relationship of a note.

* A note can be linked to **one customer only** — either a User or a Company.
* If a customer relationship already exists, it will be **replaced** with the new one provided.

This is useful for attributing feedback to the correct user or company, especially when syncing notes from support systems or CRMs.

> To remove the customer relationship completely, use the `DELETE /v2/notes/{id}/relationships/customer/{targetId}` endpoint.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note identifier.

link_user.summary: Link a user to a note

link_company.summary: Link a company to a note

delete.description:
"Deletes a relationship between a note and a target entity (customer or link).

This is used to unlink a note from:
* **customer**: A user or company associated with the note
* **link**: A specific feature, component, or other product

For customer relationships, the targetId should be the UUID of the customer or organization.
For link relationships, the targetId should be the UUID of the feature, component or product entity.

> **Note**: For customer relationships, the targetId parameter can be any UUID as it is ignored during deletion.
The endpoint will remove all customer relationships from the note regardless of the targetId value provided.
"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note identifier.

parameters.name: targetType
parameters.description:
"Relationship type to delete.
* `customer`: Delete customer relationship (user or company)
* `link`: Delete link relationship (feature, component or product entity)
"

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: UUID of the target entity (customer, organization, or link). For customer relationships, this is optional for backward compatibility.

post.description:
"Creates a new plugin integration. As part of creation, Productboard sends a
**probe request** (GET) to your configured action URL to verify it is reachable
and intends to receive action notifications. See the `callbacks` section below.

The integration is created in the `enabled` state by default. Set
`fields.integrationStatus` to `disabled` to skip the probe (useful for staged setup).

**`action` is write-only**: the action configuration, including any
`headers.authorization` secret, is never returned in responses.
"

get.description:
Sent during integration creation (and on PATCH when `enabled` and `action.url`
changes) to verify your endpoint is reachable and intends to receive action
notifications.

- Response must be `200 OK` with `Content-Type: text/plain`
- Response body must echo the `validationToken` query parameter **exactly**
- Timeout: **5 seconds**
- TLS certificate must be valid and signed by a public authority
- Failure immediately fails the create/update request with `callback.probeFailed`

This request is sent with `Accept: text/plain`.

**Security**: set `data.fields.action.headers.authorization` to have
Productboard include it in every outgoing request to your endpoint.

parameters.name: validationToken
parameters.description: Token to echo verbatim in the response body.

200.description: Echo the `validationToken` value as plain text.

post.description:
Sent when a user clicks a plugin integration button in the Productboard UI.

**Trigger types** (from `data.trigger`):
- `button.push` — user clicked to connect / push the entity
- `button.unlink` — user clicked to unlink the connected entity
- `button.dismiss` — user dismissed an error popup

Respond with a `ConnectionState` to indicate the outcome. This is equivalent
to calling the Configure Connection endpoint with the same state.

**Recommended behavior:**
- `button.push` → respond with `connected`, or `progress` for async processing
- `button.unlink` → respond with `initial` to reset the connection
- `button.dismiss` → respond with `initial` to clear the error

**Timeout: 5 seconds.** For async processing, respond with `progress`
immediately and update the connection later via the Configure Connection
endpoint (`data.links.connection` in the payload).

**Security**: set `data.fields.action.headers.authorization` to authenticate
incoming requests from Productboard.

200.description:
Respond with the resulting connection state. Any valid `ConnectionState`
is accepted. Setting `state: initial` resets the connection (equivalent
to deleting it).

Paginated using cursor-based pagination. Follow `links.next` to fetch the next page.
When `links.next` is `null`, you have reached the last page.

**OAuth2 isolation**: OAuth2 access tokens only see integrations created by the same
OAuth2 application. Public API access tokens see all workspace integrations.
"

"Returns a single plugin integration by ID.

**OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the integration was created by a different
OAuth2 application or by a public API access token (existence is masked for security).
"

patch.description:
"Partially updates a plugin integration. Fields not included in the request preserve
their current value.

If the integration is `enabled` and `fields.action.url` is updated, Productboard
sends a new probe to verify the updated endpoint. No probe is sent when `disabled`.

**`action` is write-only**: never returned in responses.
"

delete.description:
"Permanently deletes a plugin integration and all its associated connections.
Entities that previously had connections to this integration will have their
push buttons removed. This action cannot be undone.

**OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the integration was created by a different
OAuth2 application or by a public API access token.
"

get.description:
Returns all connections for a plugin integration, **excluding** those in the
`initial` state. The `initial` state is the default — it means no connection has
been established for that entity yet.

Paginated using cursor-based pagination. Follow `links.next` to fetch the next page.
When `links.next` is `null`, you have reached the last page.

"Returns the connection for a specific entity within a plugin integration.

**Important**: if no connection exists for the entity, this endpoint returns
a connection with `state: initial` rather than a `404`. The `initial` state
indicates the entity has not yet been pushed to the third-party system.
"

put.description:
"Creates or replaces the connection state for a specific entity within a plugin
integration. This is an **upsert** — if no connection exists it is created; if one
exists it is replaced.

**Connection states:**

| State | Effect |
|---|---|
| `connected` | Entity is linked; push button shows connection details |
| `error` | Connection failed; push button shows error state |
| `progress` | Processing asynchronously; push button shows loading indicator |
| `initial` | Equivalent to deleting the connection; push button resets |

**Async processing flow:**
1. User clicks push button → your endpoint receives an action notification
2. Respond immediately with `state: progress` to show a loading indicator
3. Process the action asynchronously
4. Call this endpoint with the final state (`connected` or `error`)
"

delete.description:
"Permanently deletes the connection between a entity and the third-party system,
resetting the push button to its initial unconnected state.

This is equivalent to calling the Configure Connection endpoint with `state: initial`.
"

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of teams from your workspace.
Teams represent groups of members that can be used for organizing work and assigning ownership.

- Teams are sorted by creation date, newest first.
- Use the `name` parameter to filter by name (case-insensitive exact match).
- Use the `handle` parameter to filter by handle (case-insensitive exact match).
- Use the `query` parameter to search by partial name or handle (case-insensitive). For example, `query=product` will match "Product Team", "My Product", "production", etc.
- Use the `pageCursor` parameter to paginate through results.

parameters.name: pageCursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter teams by name (case-insensitive exact match).

parameters.name: handle
parameters.description: Filter teams by handle (case-insensitive exact match).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Full-text search query. Performs case-insensitive partial match on team name and handle.

post.description:
"Creates a new team in your workspace.
You must provide the team `name` and a unique `handle` for @mentions.

## Required Fields

- `name`: The name of the team (1-255 characters, must be unique per workspace)
- `handle`: Unique handle for @mentions (lowercase alphanumeric only, 1-255 characters, must be unique per workspace)

## Response

Returns a reference to the created team with its ID and self link.
Use the GET endpoint to retrieve the full team details.
"

"## Purpose
Performs a filtered search over teams using a POST request.
Use this endpoint for batch lookups by IDs, names, or handles when query string filters are insufficient.

## Request Format

Supports two formats. Format is auto-detected by the presence of `filter` or `search` keys.

**New format** (recommended): Uses structured `filter` and `search` objects.
**Legacy format** (deprecated): Uses flat arrays directly under `data`.

## Key Features
- `filter.id`: Filter by team UUIDs (OR logic, max 100)
- `filter.fields.name`: Filter by team name, case-insensitive (single string or array, OR logic)
- `filter.fields.handle`: Filter by team handle, case-insensitive (single string or array, OR logic)
- `search.query`: Full-text search on team name and handle (case-insensitive partial match)
- Returns the same response format as `GET /v2/teams`

## Filter Logic
- Multiple values within a single filter use **OR** logic (e.g., `filter.fields.name: [\"A\", \"B\"]` returns teams named A or B)
- Different filter types and search use **AND** logic (e.g., `filter` AND `search` = intersection)
- Empty request returns all teams (same as `GET /v2/teams`)

## Important Notes
- Each filter array accepts at most 100 items; exceeding this returns a 400 error
- Pagination uses cursor-based navigation via `pageCursor` query parameter
"

parameters.name: pageCursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination.

filter_by_fields.summary: Filter by name and handle (new format)

get.description:
Retrieves the full details of a specific team by its unique identifier (UUID).
Returns the team's name, handle, description, avatar URL, and metadata such as creation and update timestamps.

Use this endpoint to load complete data for a single team.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Team identifier.

patch.description:
"Updates the fields and/or members of an existing team.
Supports both field updates via `fields` and member management via `patch` operations.
Both can be combined in a single request.

## Updatable Fields

- `name`: The name of the team (1-255 characters)
- `handle`: Unique handle for @mentions (lowercase alphanumeric only)
- `description`: Optional description of the team (max 10,000 characters)

## Patch Operations on Members

Use `patch` array to manage team members with these operations:

- `addItems`: Add members to the team (idempotent, skips already-present members)
- `removeItems`: Remove members from the team (idempotent, skips already-absent members)
- `set`: Replace entire member list atomically
- `clear`: Remove all members from the team

Maximum 100 member references per operation.

## Response

Returns a reference to the updated team.
Use the GET endpoint to retrieve the full updated team details.
"

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Team identifier.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes a team from your workspace.
This operation removes the team and all its membership associations.

**Use this endpoint with caution — deletion is irreversible.**

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Team identifier.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of members belonging to a specific team.
Members are sorted by membership creation date, newest first.

Member name and email are redacted to `[redacted]` without the `members:pii:read` scope.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Team identifier.

parameters.name: pageCursor
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination.

post.description:
"Creates a new webhook subscription to be actively notified on each change in the specified entities.

As part of subscription creation, Productboard validates the notification URL:
it must use `https`, have a publicly resolvable host, and not point to localhost,
loopback, site-local, or internal addresses.

**Scope requirements**: the OAuth2 application must have the scopes required by all requested event types.
"

Sent whenever a subscribed entity change event occurs.

- Timeout: **5 seconds** (end-to-end)
- Return any `2XX` status to acknowledge receipt
- Prefer `204 No Content` — response body is ignored
- Failed deliveries are retried with exponential backoff (up to 8 attempts: 1m, 3m, 9m, ...)
- Process notifications asynchronously and return immediately to avoid timeouts
- Too many timeouts or failures may result in Productboard **disabling the subscription** with notification loss

**Security**: configure `data.fields.notification.headers.authorization` to authenticate
incoming requests from Productboard.

200.description: Any `2XX` status is treated as a successful delivery. `204 No Content` is preferred.

get.description:
"Returns all webhook subscriptions for the workspace. Returns up to 100 items per page by default.

Paginated using cursor-based pagination. Follow `links.next` in the response to fetch the next page.
When `links.next` is `null`, you have reached the last page.

**OAuth2 isolation**: OAuth2 access tokens only see subscriptions created by the same OAuth2 application.
Public API access tokens see all workspace subscriptions.
"

200.description: A paginated list of webhook subscriptions

get.description:
"Returns a single webhook subscription by ID.

**OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the subscription was created by a different OAuth2 application
or by a public API access token (existence is masked for security).
"

delete.description:
"Permanently deletes a webhook subscription. No further notifications will be sent after deletion. **OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the subscription was created by a different OAuth2 application
or by a public API access token.
"

"Date and time in ISO 8601 format for Entities.

## Behavior

pointer.description: JSON Pointer [RFC6901] to the associated entity in the request document.

parameter.description:
Query parameter causing the error.
Used when the error is related to a query parameter rather than request body.

ErrorSource.description: An object containing references to the source of the error. It helps with location of the problematic field or parameter.

code.description:
"A unique, machine-readable and stable code that identifies this error.
Must be consistent across all services and provide clear categorization.
Format: `category.subcategory.specificError`
"

title.description:
A short, human-readable summary of the problem that doesn't change
from occurrence to occurrence of the problem. Should provide immediate
understanding of the error type.

detail.description:
A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
May include suggestions for resolution but should not expose sensitive information.

Error.description: Individual error object containing the details and context.

id.description:
A unique identifier for this specific occurrence of the problem.
It carries unique request/response ID which allows client
and server to reference the exact instance of the error
in logs or support communication.

errors.description: Array of error objects, multiple errors can be returned.

Entity.description: The entity payload containing the resource data and its relationships.

EntityFieldValue.description:
A list of possible value types of PM entity fields.
This should cover all possible fields of all PM entity types.

EntityCreateOrUpdateFields.description:
"A collection of fields that can be used to create or update a PM entity.
It is a subset of `EntityFields` that is used for creation or update operations.

## Dual Format Support
Most field types support two input formats:
- **Simple format**: Direct value (e.g., `\"text\"`, `123`, `true`)
- **Object format**: Value with metadata (e.g., `{\"value\": \"text\", \"metadata\": {...}}`)

## Metadata Support
Custom fields (identified by UUID) and specific field types (`domain`, `email`, `effort`) support 
optional metadata that allows tracking the external source of field values:
- `source.system`: Name of the external system that provided the value
- `source.recordId`: Identifier of the record in the external system
- `isViewableOnly`: Whether the value is read-only from external source

Metadata sent for other fields is ignored.
"

EntityCreateOrUpdateFieldValue.description: A list of create/update payloads for possible value types of a resource fields.

EntityPatch.description:
A list of patch operations to perform on a PM entity field.
Each operation can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`, or `clear`.

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`.

path.description:
The path to the field to be modified.
It is usually a field id (eg. "tags", "owner", "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000").

EntityPatchOperation.description: This patch operation replaces the value of a field, or adds/removes values from it.

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `clear`.

path.description:
The path to the field to be modified.
It is usually a field id (eg. "tags", "owner", "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000").

EntityPatchOperationClear.description: This patch operation clears the value of a field.

fields.description: **(Legacy, deprecated)** Use `return.fields` instead.

"Request body for searching entities. Supports two formats:

**New format** (recommended): Uses a structured `filter` object with nested field groups.
**Legacy format** (deprecated): Uses flat properties directly under `data`.

Both formats are accepted. The legacy format will continue to work but is deprecated
in favour of the new structured format. Format is detected by the presence of the `filter` key.
"

type.description: Filter by one or more entity types. If empty or not provided, searches across all entity types.

EntitySearchFilter.description: Filter criteria for the entity search.

name.description:
Entity names and titles for Entities with a 255 character limit.

## Behavior
- Accepts any string content up to 255 characters

fields.description:
"Controls which fields are returned in the response to optimize bandwidth and tailor data to your needs.

**Options:**
- Not specified or `null`: Returns only fields with non-empty values
- `[\"all\"]`: Returns all fields, including those with `null` values
- `[\"name\", \"status\", ...]`: Returns only the specified fields

For detailed usage, examples, and field discovery, see [Response Field Control](https://developer.productboard.com/v2.0.0/reference/response-field-control).
"

EntitySearchReturn.description: Controls which fields are included in the response.

fields.description:
A key-value definition fields that are part of the entity.
Key represents field id (eg. name, description, or UUID)

relationships.description:
A key-value definition of possible relationships to other entities.
Key represents the relationship type to other entities (child, parent, link)

filters.description:
Available filter parameters for this entity type.
Use these as query parameters on `GET /v2/entities` or as keys in `POST /v2/entities/search` request body.
Not all entity types support the same filters — this list is type-specific.

name.description:
The query parameter name to use when filtering.
Use this exact name as a query parameter on `GET /v2/entities`
or as a key in the `POST /v2/entities/search` request body.

field.description: The field ID this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a field.

relationship.description: The relationship key this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a relationship.

requiredScopes.description:
OAuth2 scopes required to use this filter,
beyond the standard entity scopes. Omitted when no additional scopes are needed.

ConfigurationFilterParameter.description:
Describes a single filter parameter available for an entity type.
The `name` is the exact query parameter name to use when filtering entities.

type.description: The value type expected for this filter.

format.description: Additional format hint for the value.

ConfigurationFilterSchema.description: Describes the expected value type and format for a filter parameter.

cardinality.description: Whether the entity can have single or multiple relationships of a given type

mandatory.description:
Specifies whether the entity must have such relationship to a target entity (e.g. it must have parent).
If a relationship is mandatory and can have multiple entity types as targets, then there must exist *at least
one* relationship to an entity that is of any of the specified types.

For example, a feature must have a parent that is either a component, or a product.

ConfigurationFieldLifecycle.description:
Defines the available operations for a field during different lifecycle stages.
Possible lifecycle stages are `create`, `update` and `patch`.
  - `create` defines what operations are allowed when creating an entity
  - `update` defines what operations are allowed when using a simple patch via `data.fields`
  - `patch` defines what operations are allowed when using a JSON Patch via `data.patch` array

set.description: Whether the field value can be set (assigned).

clear.description: Whether the field value can be cleared (set to null/empty).

addItems.description: Whether items can be added to the field (for list-type fields).

removeItems.description: Whether items can be removed from the field (for list-type fields).

ConfigurationFieldLifecycleOperation.description: Defines the specific operations allowed during a lifecycle stage.

maxSize.description: Maximum size in bytes for content fields.

maxItems.description: Maximum number of items for list-type fields.

allowEmpty.description: Whether empty string values are allowed.

uniqueItems.description: Whether items in list-type fields must be unique.

ConfigurationFieldConstraints.description: Validation constraints and rules for the field.

ConfigurationFieldConstraintsValues.description: Defines constraints on allowed values for the field.

list.description: URL endpoint that provides the list of allowed values.

ConfigurationFieldConstraintsQuery.description: Query-based value constraints referencing external endpoints.

ConfigurationFieldDefaultValue.description: Default value configuration for the field.

data.description:
"Inline value options. Can be empty.
For small sets: contains all values.
For large sets: contains preview or empty.
"

next.description:
URL to fetch values. Present only when more values exist
beyond what's in `data`. Supports pagination via `pageCursor`.

FieldValues.description:
Configuration for field values. Provides inline data for small sets
and/or a reference link for large/dynamic sets.

Interpretation:
- `data` only → Complete set inline
- `data` + `links.next` → Preview in data, full list at link
- `data` empty + `links.next` → Fully dynamic, fetch from link

name.description: Display name of the value.

assignedEntityTypes.description: Entity types this value is assigned to. Only present for status field values.

FieldValueItem.description: A single allowed value for a select-type or status field.

id.description:
The unique identifier of the field. For simple fields like "name", 
for nested fields like "timeframe.startDate", for UUID custom fields.

name.description: The display name of the field.

path.description: The path used for API operations. Uses "/fields/" prefix and nested paths for properties.

schema.description: Inline JSON Schema that defines this field's structure.

lifecycle.description:
Defines the available operations for a field during different lifecycle stages.
Possible lifecycle stages are `create`, `update` and `patch`.
  - `create` defines what operations are allowed when creating an entity
  - `update` defines what operations are allowed when using a simple patch via `data.fields`
  - `patch` defines what operations are allowed when using a JSON Patch via `data.patch` array

ConfigurationField.description:
Field configuration with inline JSON schema and path for API operations.
This structure is used for all fields, including nested properties.

EntityRelationships.description:
A collection of relationships between this entity and other entities.
The list is paginated, to see the rest of the relationships follow the URL contained in the
`links.next` attribute.

EntityRelationship.description:
Represents a relationship between two entities.
The source entity is implicit from the request context (the entity being queried).

EntityRelationshipType.description:
"The type of the relationship to be established.

## Relationships
- `parent`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity is a child of the target
- `child`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity contains the target
- `link`: Generic bidirectional relationship without semantic meaning
- `isBlockedBy`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity cannot proceed until the target is resolved
- `isBlocking`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity prevents progress on the target
"

The exact types available may vary based on the configuration of the workspace.

"Calendar date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for Entities.

TimeframeFieldValue.description:
"Time period with start and end dates and granularity level for Entities.

This object contains:
- A `startDate` marking the beginning of the timeframe
- An `endDate` marking the conclusion of the timeframe
- A `granularity` indicating the time precision level (`year`, `quarter`, `month`, `day`)

## Behavior
- Both `startDate` and `endDate` use ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `endDate` must be on or after `startDate`
- `granularity` affects how dates are displayed and interpreted in the UI
- All fields are optional, allowing partial timeframe specification
- Used for planning, scheduling, time-based filtering, and defining work periods for initiatives, features, and releases
- Supports different planning granularities from daily sprints to quarterly planning

## Examples

**Monthly planning timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-01-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"month\"
}
```

**Quarterly initiative timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-03-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"quarter\"
}
```

**Annual strategic timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-12-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"year\"
}
```

**Sprint timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-15\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-01-29\",
  \"granularity\": \"day\"
}
```
"

HealthFieldValue.description:
"Health status of a Entities with tracking metadata and history.

This object contains:
- A unique `id` for the health record
- The current `status` (`notSet`, `onTrack`, `atRisk`, `offTrack`)
- The calculation `mode` (`manual` or `calculated`)
- Timestamp and user tracking information
- Optional comment providing context
- Previous status for change tracking

## Behavior
- The `status` field indicates current health state
- The `mode` field determines how health is set:
  - `manual`: Status explicitly set by users with optional comments
  - `calculated`: Status automatically derived from system logic or child entities
- `lastUpdatedAt` is automatically maintained by the system
- `previousStatus` tracks the last health state for change detection
- `createdBy` identifies who set the health status (for manual mode)
- Read-only field populated by the system after health updates
- Used to monitor work item health across the product hierarchy and track status changes over time

## Examples

**Manual health with comment:**
```json
{
  \"id\": \"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000\",
  \"status\": \"atRisk\",
  \"mode\": \"manual\",
  \"lastUpdatedAt\": \"2024-01-15T10:30:00Z\",
  \"comment\": \"Resource constraints affecting timeline\",
  \"createdBy\": {
    \"email\": \"manager@company.com\"
  },
  \"previousStatus\": \"onTrack\"
}
```

**Calculated health:**
```json
{
  \"id\": \"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001\",
  \"status\": \"onTrack\",
  \"mode\": \"calculated\",
  \"lastUpdatedAt\": \"2024-01-15T15:45:00Z\",
  \"previousStatus\": \"notSet\"
}
```
"

comment.description:
"HTML-formatted text content for Entities.

  - Follow [Rich Text Formatting guidelines](https://developer.productboard.com/reference/rich-text-formatting)
- Used for detailed descriptions, specifications, documentation, and formatted comments
"

HealthUpdateFieldValue.description:
"Represents the health status of a Entities, either manually set or automatically calculated.

This object contains:
- The mode of health tracking (`manual` or `calculated`)
- The health `status` (required only when mode is `manual`)
- An optional `comment` to provide context
- Metadata on who created the update (`createdBy`)

### Behavior
- If `mode` is `\"manual\"`, the `status` field is **required**, and you may include an optional `comment`.
- If `mode` is `\"calculated\"`, **do not provide** the `status` or `comment` fields – they will be ignored or cause validation errors.

### Use cases
- Set `manual` mode to explicitly assign a health status with reasoning.
- Use `calculated` mode when health is derived from internal logic or automation within Productboard.

### Examples

**Manual mode:**
```json
{
  \"mode\": \"manual\",
  \"status\": \"atRisk\",
  \"comment\": \"Project timeline at risk due to resource constraints\"
}
```

**Calculated mode:**
```json
{
  \"mode\": \"calculated\"
}
```
"

startValue.description: The starting baseline value for progress measurement.

targetValue.description: The target goal value to achieve.

currentValue.description: The current progress value being tracked.

ProgressFieldValue.description:
"Measurable progress tracking for key results with start, target, and current values.

This object contains:
- A `startValue` representing the baseline measurement
- A `targetValue` representing the goal to achieve
- A `currentValue` representing the present measurement

## Behavior
- All values are floating-point numbers allowing decimal precision
- Values can represent various metrics (percentages, counts, amounts, etc.)
- The `currentValue` is updated to track progress toward the `targetValue`
- Progress calculation: `(currentValue - startValue) / (targetValue - startValue) * 100`
- Supports both positive and negative progress directions
- Currently supported only for key results
- Used to track quantifiable metrics like revenue, users, and performance indicators

## Examples

**Percentage-based progress:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 0.0,
  \"targetValue\": 100.0,
  \"currentValue\": 75.5
}
```

**Revenue tracking:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 50000.0,
  \"targetValue\": 100000.0,
  \"currentValue\": 87500.0
}
```

**User count growth:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 1000.0,
  \"targetValue\": 5000.0,
  \"currentValue\": 3250.0
}
```
"

startValue.description: The starting baseline value for progress measurement.

targetValue.description: The target goal value to achieve.

currentValue.description: The current progress value being tracked.

ProgressFieldValueWithMetadata.description: Progress value with metadata about its source and visibility.

value.description: The work progress percentage value between 0 and 100 (inclusive).

WorkProgressFieldValue.description:
"Work progress percentage for Entities, tracking completion status from 0-100%.

This object contains:
- A numeric `value` representing completion percentage (0-100)
- A `mode` indicating how the progress is calculated (`manual`, `statusBased`, or `calculated`)

## Behavior
- The `value` field must be an integer between 0 and 100 (inclusive)
- The `mode` field determines how progress is tracked and updated:
  - `manual`: Progress is set directly by users
  - `statusBased`: Progress is automatically calculated based on entity status
  - `calculated`: Progress is derived from child entity relationships (parent/link)
- Different entity types support different modes:
  - **subfeature**: `manual`, `statusBased`
  - **feature**: `manual`, `statusBased`, `calculated`
  - **initiative**: `manual`, `calculated`
  - **objective**: `manual`, `calculated`
- When mode is `calculated`, the system automatically updates the value based on relationships
- Used to track completion percentage across the product hierarchy and monitor progress aggregation

## Examples

**Manual progress:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 75,
  \"mode\": \"manual\"
}
```

**Status-based progress:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 50,
  \"mode\": \"statusBased\"
}
```

**Calculated progress from relationships:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 85,
  \"mode\": \"calculated\"
}
```
"

WorkProgressModeEnum.description: The mode of work progress calculation.

fixed_EntityCreateOrUpdateFields.description:
"A collection of fields that can be used to create or update a PM entity.
It is a subset of `EntityFields` that is used for creation or update operations.

## Dual Format Support
Most field types support two input formats:
- **Simple format**: Direct value (e.g., `\"text\"`, `123`, `true`)
- **Object format**: Value with metadata (e.g., `{\"value\": \"text\", \"metadata\": {...}}`)

## Metadata Support
Custom fields (identified by UUID) and specific field types (`domain`, `email`, `effort`) support 
optional metadata that allows tracking the external source of field values:
- `source.system`: Name of the external system that provided the value
- `source.recordId`: Identifier of the record in the external system
- `isViewableOnly`: Whether the value is read-only from external source

Metadata sent for other fields is ignored.
"

or as a key in the `POST /v2/entities/search` request body.

field.description: The field ID this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a field.

relationship.description: The relationship key this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a relationship.

requiredScopes.description:
OAuth2 scopes required to use this filter,
beyond the standard entity scopes. Omitted when no additional scopes are needed.

fixed_ConfigurationFilterParameter.description:
Describes a single filter parameter available for an entity type.
The `name` is the exact query parameter name to use when filtering entities.

type.description: The value type expected for this filter.

format.description: Additional format hint for the value.

fixed_ConfigurationFilterSchema.description: Describes the expected value type and format for a filter parameter.

cardinality.description: Whether the entity can have single or multiple relationships of a given type

mandatory.description:
Specifies whether the entity must have such relationship to a target entity (e.g. it must have parent).
If a relationship is mandatory and can have multiple entity types as targets, then there must exist *at least
one* relationship to an entity that is of any of the specified types.

For example, a feature must have a parent that is either a component, or a product.

fixed_ConfigurationFieldLifecycle.description:
Defines the available operations for a field during different lifecycle stages.
Possible lifecycle stages are `create`, `update` and `patch`.
  - `create` defines what operations are allowed when creating an entity
  - `update` defines what operations are allowed when using a simple patch via `data.fields`
  - `patch` defines what operations are allowed when using a JSON Patch via `data.patch` array

set.description: Whether the field value can be set (assigned).

clear.description: Whether the field value can be cleared (set to null/empty).

addItems.description: Whether items can be added to the field (for list-type fields).

removeItems.description: Whether items can be removed from the field (for list-type fields).

fixed_ConfigurationFieldLifecycleOperati.description: Defines the specific operations allowed during a lifecycle stage.

maxSize.description: Maximum size in bytes for content fields.

maxItems.description: Maximum number of items for list-type fields.

allowEmpty.description: Whether empty string values are allowed.

uniqueItems.description: Whether items in list-type fields must be unique.

fixed_ConfigurationFieldConstraints.description: Validation constraints and rules for the field.

fixed_ConfigurationFieldConstraintsValue.description: Defines constraints on allowed values for the field.

list.description: URL endpoint that provides the list of allowed values.

fixed_ConfigurationFieldConstraintsQuery.description: Query-based value constraints referencing external endpoints.

fixed_ConfigurationFieldDefaultValue.description: Default value configuration for the field.

data.description:
"Inline value options. Can be empty.
For small sets: contains all values.
For large sets: contains preview or empty.
"

next.description:
URL to fetch values. Present only when more values exist
beyond what's in `data`. Supports pagination via `pageCursor`.

fixed_FieldValues.description:
Configuration for field values. Provides inline data for small sets
and/or a reference link for large/dynamic sets.

Interpretation:
- `data` only → Complete set inline
- `data` + `links.next` → Preview in data, full list at link
- `data` empty + `links.next` → Fully dynamic, fetch from link

name.description: Display name of the value.

assignedEntityTypes.description: Entity types this value is assigned to. Only present for status field values.

fixed_FieldValueItem.description: A single allowed value for a select-type or status field.

for nested fields like "timeframe.startDate", for UUID custom fields.

path.description: The path used for API operations. Uses "/fields/" prefix and nested paths for properties.

schema.description: Inline JSON Schema that defines this field's structure.

lifecycle.description:
Defines the available operations for a field during different lifecycle stages.
Possible lifecycle stages are `create`, `update` and `patch`.
  - `create` defines what operations are allowed when creating an entity
  - `update` defines what operations are allowed when using a simple patch via `data.fields`
  - `patch` defines what operations are allowed when using a JSON Patch via `data.patch` array

fixed_EntityRelationships.description:
A collection of relationships between this entity and other entities.
The list is paginated, to see the rest of the relationships follow the URL contained in the

fixed_EntityRelationship.description:
Represents a relationship between two entities.
The source entity is implicit from the request context (the entity being queried).

fixed_EntityRelationshipType.description:
"The type of the relationship to be established.

## Relationships
- `parent`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity is a child of the target
- `child`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity contains the target
- `link`: Generic bidirectional relationship without semantic meaning
- `isBlockedBy`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity cannot proceed until the target is resolved
- `isBlocking`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity prevents progress on the target

The exact types available may vary based on the configuration of the workspace.

fixed_TimeframeFieldValue.description:
"Time period with start and end dates and granularity level for Entities.

This object contains:
- A `startDate` marking the beginning of the timeframe
- An `endDate` marking the conclusion of the timeframe
- A `granularity` indicating the time precision level (`year`, `quarter`, `month`, `day`)

## Behavior
- Both `startDate` and `endDate` use ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `endDate` must be on or after `startDate`
- `granularity` affects how dates are displayed and interpreted in the UI
- All fields are optional, allowing partial timeframe specification
- Used for planning, scheduling, time-based filtering, and defining work periods for initiatives, features, and releases
- Supports different planning granularities from daily sprints to quarterly planning

## Examples

**Monthly planning timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-01-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"month\"
}
```

**Quarterly initiative timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-03-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"quarter\"
}
```

**Annual strategic timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-12-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"year\"
}
```

**Sprint timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-15\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-01-29\",
  \"granularity\": \"day\"
}
```
"

fixed_HealthFieldValue.description:
"Health status of a Entities with tracking metadata and history.

This object contains:
- A unique `id` for the health record
- The current `status` (`notSet`, `onTrack`, `atRisk`, `offTrack`)
- The calculation `mode` (`manual` or `calculated`)
- Timestamp and user tracking information
- Optional comment providing context
- Previous status for change tracking

## Behavior
- The `status` field indicates current health state
- The `mode` field determines how health is set:
  - `manual`: Status explicitly set by users with optional comments
  - `calculated`: Status automatically derived from system logic or child entities
- `lastUpdatedAt` is automatically maintained by the system
- `previousStatus` tracks the last health state for change detection
- `createdBy` identifies who set the health status (for manual mode)
- Read-only field populated by the system after health updates
- Used to monitor work item health across the product hierarchy and track status changes over time

## Examples

**Manual health with comment:**
```json
{
  \"id\": \"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000\",
  \"status\": \"atRisk\",
  \"mode\": \"manual\",
  \"lastUpdatedAt\": \"2024-01-15T10:30:00Z\",
  \"comment\": \"Resource constraints affecting timeline\",
  \"createdBy\": {
    \"email\": \"manager@company.com\"
  },
  \"previousStatus\": \"onTrack\"
}
```

**Calculated health:**
```json
{
  \"id\": \"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001\",
  \"status\": \"onTrack\",
  \"mode\": \"calculated\",
  \"lastUpdatedAt\": \"2024-01-15T15:45:00Z\",
  \"previousStatus\": \"notSet\"
}
```
"

comment.description:
"HTML-formatted text content for Entities.

## Behavior
- Accepts HTML-formatted strings up to 524KB (524,288 characters)

  - Follow [Rich Text Formatting guidelines](https://developer.productboard.com/reference/rich-text-formatting)
- Used for detailed descriptions, specifications, documentation, and formatted comments
"

fixed_HealthUpdateFieldValue.description:
"Represents the health status of a Entities, either manually set or automatically calculated.

This object contains:
- The mode of health tracking (`manual` or `calculated`)
- The health `status` (required only when mode is `manual`)
- An optional `comment` to provide context
- Metadata on who created the update (`createdBy`)

### Behavior
- If `mode` is `\"manual\"`, the `status` field is **required**, and you may include an optional `comment`.
- If `mode` is `\"calculated\"`, **do not provide** the `status` or `comment` fields – they will be ignored or cause validation errors.

### Use cases
- Set `manual` mode to explicitly assign a health status with reasoning.
- Use `calculated` mode when health is derived from internal logic or automation within Productboard.

### Examples

**Manual mode:**
```json
{
  \"mode\": \"manual\",
  \"status\": \"atRisk\",
  \"comment\": \"Project timeline at risk due to resource constraints\"
}
```

**Calculated mode:**
```json
{
  \"mode\": \"calculated\"
}
```
"

startValue.description: The starting baseline value for progress measurement.

fixed_ProgressFieldValue.description:
"Measurable progress tracking for key results with start, target, and current values.

This object contains:
- A `startValue` representing the baseline measurement
- A `targetValue` representing the goal to achieve
- A `currentValue` representing the present measurement

## Behavior
- All values are floating-point numbers allowing decimal precision
- Values can represent various metrics (percentages, counts, amounts, etc.)
- The `currentValue` is updated to track progress toward the `targetValue`
- Progress calculation: `(currentValue - startValue) / (targetValue - startValue) * 100`
- Supports both positive and negative progress directions
- Currently supported only for key results
- Used to track quantifiable metrics like revenue, users, and performance indicators

## Examples

**Percentage-based progress:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 0.0,
  \"targetValue\": 100.0,
  \"currentValue\": 75.5
}
```

**Revenue tracking:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 50000.0,
  \"targetValue\": 100000.0,
  \"currentValue\": 87500.0
}
```

**User count growth:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 1000.0,
  \"targetValue\": 5000.0,
  \"currentValue\": 3250.0
}
```
"

startValue.description: The starting baseline value for progress measurement.

value.description: The work progress percentage value between 0 and 100 (inclusive).

fixed_WorkProgressFieldValue.description:
"Work progress percentage for Entities, tracking completion status from 0-100%.

This object contains:
- A numeric `value` representing completion percentage (0-100)
- A `mode` indicating how the progress is calculated (`manual`, `statusBased`, or `calculated`)

## Behavior
- The `value` field must be an integer between 0 and 100 (inclusive)
- The `mode` field determines how progress is tracked and updated:
  - `manual`: Progress is set directly by users
  - `statusBased`: Progress is automatically calculated based on entity status
  - `calculated`: Progress is derived from child entity relationships (parent/link)
- Different entity types support different modes:
  - **subfeature**: `manual`, `statusBased`
  - **feature**: `manual`, `statusBased`, `calculated`
  - **initiative**: `manual`, `calculated`
  - **objective**: `manual`, `calculated`
- When mode is `calculated`, the system automatically updates the value based on relationships
- Used to track completion percentage across the product hierarchy and monitor progress aggregation

## Examples

**Manual progress:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 75,
  \"mode\": \"manual\"
}
```

**Status-based progress:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 50,
  \"mode\": \"statusBased\"
}
```

**Calculated progress from relationships:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 85,
  \"mode\": \"calculated\"
}
```
"

MemberSearchData.description:
Search data that accepts both new structured format and legacy flat format.
If `filter`, `search`, or `return` is present, the new format is used. Otherwise, the legacy flat format is assumed.

MemberSearchFilter.description:
Structured filters for member search. Different filter groups use AND logic.
All filters are optional.

email.description:
Filter by email address. Single string or array (OR logic), case-insensitive.
Requires `members_pii:read` scope.

role.description: Filter by role. Single string or array (OR logic).

disabled.description:
Exclusive filter for disabled members. When `true`, returns **only** disabled members.
When absent, disabled members are excluded by default.
Use `return.includeDisabled` instead if you want disabled members alongside active ones.

invitationPending.description:
Exclusive filter for members with pending invitations. When `true`, returns **only** pending members.
When absent, pending members are excluded by default.
Use `return.includeInvitationPending` instead if you want pending members alongside accepted ones.

MemberSearchFilterFields.description:
Field-level filters. Multiple values within a field use OR logic.
Email matching is case-insensitive. Unknown fields return a 400 error.

query.description:
Full-text search query. Performs case-insensitive partial match on member name or email.
Requires `members_pii:read` scope.

MemberSearchSearch.description: Full-text search options. Combined with filters using AND logic.

includeDisabled.description:
Include disabled members alongside active members. Default false.
If `filter.fields.disabled` is also set, the filter takes precedence.

includeInvitationPending.description:
Include members with pending invitations alongside accepted members. Default false.
If `filter.fields.invitationPending` is also set, the filter takes precedence.

MemberSearchReturn.description: Controls inclusion of normally-excluded members in the response.

role.description: Role of the member in the workspace.

invitationPending.description: Whether the member has a pending invitation that has not yet been accepted.

NotePatch.description:
"A list of patch operations to perform on a Note field.
Each operation can be one of `set`, `clear`, `addItems`, or `removeItems`.

**Supported Operations:**
- `set`: Set a field to a specific value (supports all fields)
- `clear`: Clear/reset a field to its default value (supports owner, tags, content)
- `addItems`: Add items to array fields (supports tags, conversation content)
- `removeItems`: Remove items from array fields (supports tags, conversation content)

**Field-specific support:**
- **owner**: set, clear
- **tags**: set, clear, addItems, removeItems
- **archived**: set
- **processed**: set
- **name**: set
- **content** (textNote): set
- **content** (conversationNote): set, addItems, removeItems

**Operation Compatibility Rules (for each field):**
- Cannot combine `set`/`clear` with `addItems`/`removeItems` operations on the same field
- Cannot combine both `set` and `clear` operations on the same field
- Can combine `addItems` and `removeItems` together on the same field (for array operations)
"

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`.

path.description:
"The path to the field to be modified.
Common field paths: \"owner\", \"tags\", \"archived\", \"processed\", \"name\", \"content\"
"

NotePatchOperation.description:
"This patch operation modifies a field value using set, addItems, or removeItems operations.

**Operation Types:**
- `set`: Replace the entire field value
- `addItems`: Add items to array fields (tags, conversation content)
- `removeItems`: Remove items from array fields (tags, conversation content)
"

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Must be `clear`.

path.description:
"The path to the field to be cleared.
Common field paths: \"owner\", \"tags\", \"content\"
"

NotePatchOperationClear.description:
"This patch operation clears/resets the value of a field to its default state.

**Supported Fields:**
- `owner`: Clears the owner (sets to null)
- `tags`: Clears all tags (sets to empty array)
- `content`: Clears content (for conversationNote/opportunityNote notes)
"

- `Bearer <token>`

PluginIntegrationConnection.description:
A plugin integration connection resource, representing the relationship between
a Productboard entity and an entity in the third-party system.

The `id` is the Productboard entity ID. The connection is scoped to a specific
plugin integration identified in the URL path.

PluginIntegrationConnectionFields.description: Domain attributes of a plugin integration connection.

self.description: URL to retrieve this connection.

id.description: The Productboard entity ID this connection belongs to.

PluginIntegrationConnectionReference.description: Minimal reference to a plugin integration connection, returned in mutation responses.

ConnectionState.description:
The state of a plugin integration connection. Determined by the `state` property.

| State | Meaning | Push button appearance |
|---|---|---|
| `connected` | Entity linked to third-party entity | Shows `label`; click opens `targetUrl` |
| `error` | Connection attempt failed | Shows error icon with `message` |
| `progress` | Establishing asynchronously | Shows loading indicator |
| `initial` | No connection (default) | Shows integration's `initialState.label` |

label.description: Text on the push button when connected. Typically the entity's current status.

hoverLabel.description: Text on the push button on hover (temporarily replaces `label`). Typically shows the linked entity's identifier.

tooltip.description: Longer tooltip text shown on hover. Ideal for a detailed description of the linked entity.

color.description: Background color of the push button.

targetUrl.description: URL that opens when the user clicks the connected push button. Should link to the connected entity in the third-party system.

ConnectedState.description:
Represents a successfully established connection. The push button displays custom
label, hover text, tooltip, color, and a link to the connected entity.

message.description: Human-readable error message explaining why the connection could not be established. Shown in the error popup in the Productboard UI.
message.example: You don't have permission to create an issue.

debugDetail.description:
Additional debugging information set by Productboard when a push fails on
Productboard's side. **Not visible in the UI** — for developer use during
integration development only. Never set by the partner.
debugDetail.example: JSON decoding error: field 'targetUrl' is missing

ErrorState.description: Represents a failed connection attempt. The push button displays an error icon with the provided message.

ProgressState.description:
"Represents an in-progress connection attempt. The push button shows a loading
indicator.

Use when processing is asynchronous: respond with `progress` immediately from your
action endpoint, then call Configure Connection with the final state once done.
"

InitialConnectionState.description:
Represents the default, unconnected state. The push button shows the integration's
`initialState.label`. Setting a connection to `initial` via Configure Connection
is equivalent to deleting it.
