handle.description: Unique handle for @mentions. Lowercase alphanumeric only.

avatarUrl.description:
URL of the team's avatar image. Read-only.
Returns `null` if no avatar is set.

TeamMemberResource.description:
A team member with fields and links.
Name and email are redacted to [redacted] without the members:pii:read scope.

name.description:
The name of the team.

# Validation
- Required for creation
- Must be between 1 and 255 characters
- Must be unique per workspace

# Filtering
Supports exact match via `name` parameter and partial match via `query` parameter on GET /v2/teams.

handle.description:
Unique handle for @mentions.

# Validation
- Required for creation
- Lowercase letters and numbers only (no uppercase, spaces, or special characters)
- Must be unique per workspace

description.description:
"Optional description of the team.

# Validation
- Optional field
- Maximum length: 10000 characters
"

TeamCreateOrUpdateFields.description:
Fields for creating or updating a team.
For creation, `name` and `handle` are required.
For updates, the `fields` object is required but all individual field properties within it are optional.

**Note:** `avatarUrl` is read-only and cannot be set via API. Avatar management is only available through the UI.

path.description: The field to operate on.

value.description:
Member references for the operation.
Each item must include either `id` (space membership UUID) or `email`.

oneOf.description: Operation with a value (addItems, removeItems, set)

op.description: Clear all items from the field.

path.description: The field to clear.

TeamPatchOperation.description: A patch operation for managing list-type fields on a team.

TeamSearchRequest.description: Request body for searching teams with filters.

ids.description: (Legacy) Filter by team UUIDs (OR logic). Use `filter.id` instead.

names.description: (Legacy) Filter by team names, case-insensitive (OR logic). Use `filter.fields.name` instead.

handles.description: (Legacy) Filter by team handles, case-insensitive (OR logic). Use `filter.fields.handle` instead.

TeamSearchData.description:
Search data that accepts both new structured format and legacy flat format.
If `filter` or `search` is present, the new format is used. Otherwise, the legacy flat format is assumed.

TeamSearchFilter.description:
Structured filters for team search. Different filter groups use AND logic.
All filters are optional.

name.description: Filter by team name. Single string or array (OR logic), case-insensitive.

handle.description: Filter by team handle. Single string or array (OR logic), case-insensitive.

TeamSearchFilterFields.description:
Field-level filters. Multiple values within a field use OR logic.
String matching is case-insensitive.

query.description: Full-text search query. Performs case-insensitive partial match on team name and handle.

TeamSearchSearch.description: Full-text search options. Combined with filters using AND logic.

members_MemberSearchData.description:
Search data that accepts both new structured format and legacy flat format.
If `filter`, `search`, or `return` is present, the new format is used. Otherwise, the legacy flat format is assumed.

members_MemberSearchFilter.description:
Structured filters for member search. Different filter groups use AND logic.
All filters are optional.

email.description:
Filter by email address. Single string or array (OR logic), case-insensitive.
Requires `members_pii:read` scope.

role.description: Filter by role. Single string or array (OR logic).

disabled.description:
Exclusive filter for disabled members. When `true`, returns **only** disabled members.
When absent, disabled members are excluded by default.
Use `return.includeDisabled` instead if you want disabled members alongside active ones.

invitationPending.description:
Exclusive filter for members with pending invitations. When `true`, returns **only** pending members.
When absent, pending members are excluded by default.
Use `return.includeInvitationPending` instead if you want pending members alongside accepted ones.

members_MemberSearchFilterFields.description:
Field-level filters. Multiple values within a field use OR logic.
Email matching is case-insensitive. Unknown fields return a 400 error.

query.description:
Full-text search query. Performs case-insensitive partial match on member name or email.
Requires `members_pii:read` scope.

members_MemberSearchSearch.description: Full-text search options. Combined with filters using AND logic.

includeDisabled.description:
Include disabled members alongside active members. Default false.
If `filter.fields.disabled` is also set, the filter takes precedence.

includeInvitationPending.description:
Include members with pending invitations alongside accepted members. Default false.
If `filter.fields.invitationPending` is also set, the filter takes precedence.

members_MemberSearchReturn.description: Controls inclusion of normally-excluded members in the response.

members_Member.description: Represents a member (user) in the workspace.

role.description: Role of the member in the workspace.

invitationPending.description: Whether the member has a pending invitation that has not yet been accepted.

teams.description: List of teams the member belongs to. Always included in the response.

merged_openapi_EntityFieldValue.description:
A list of possible value types of PM entity fields.
This should cover all possible fields of all PM entity types.

merged_openapi_EntityCreateOrUpdateField.description:
"A collection of fields that can be used to create or update a PM entity.
It is a subset of `EntityFields` that is used for creation or update operations.

## Dual Format Support
Most field types support two input formats:
- **Simple format**: Direct value (e.g., `\"text\"`, `123`, `true`)
- **Object format**: Value with metadata (e.g., `{\"value\": \"text\", \"metadata\": {...}}`)

## Metadata Support
Custom fields (identified by UUID) and specific field types (`domain`, `email`, `effort`) support 
optional metadata that allows tracking the external source of field values:
- `source.system`: Name of the external system that provided the value
- `source.recordId`: Identifier of the record in the external system
- `isViewableOnly`: Whether the value is read-only from external source

Metadata sent for other fields is ignored.
"
A list of create/update payloads for possible value types of a resource fields.

merged_openapi_EntityPatch.description:
A list of patch operations to perform on a PM entity field.
Each operation can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`, or `clear`.

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`.

path.description:
The path to the field to be modified.
It is usually a field id (eg. "tags", "owner", "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000").

merged_openapi_EntityPatchOperation.description: This patch operation replaces the value of a field, or adds/removes values from it.

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `clear`.

path.description:
The path to the field to be modified.
It is usually a field id (eg. "tags", "owner", "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000").

merged_openapi_EntityPatchOperationClear.description: This patch operation clears the value of a field.

- Accepts any string content up to 255 characters
- Can be empty string, though typically entity names are required
- Supports UTF-8 encoding for international characters
- Used for entity titles, display labels, and primary identifiers

merged_openapi_EntitySearch.description:
"Request body for searching entities. Supports two formats:

**New format** (recommended): Uses a structured `filter` object with nested field groups.
**Legacy format** (deprecated): Uses flat properties directly under `data`.

Both formats are accepted. The legacy format will continue to work but is deprecated
in favour of the new structured format. Format is detected by the presence of the `filter` key.
"

merged_openapi_DateRangeFilter.description: A date range filter with optional `from` and `to` bounds.

fields.description:
"Controls which fields are returned in the response to optimize bandwidth and tailor data to your needs.

**Options:**
- Not specified or `null`: Returns only fields with non-empty values
- `[\"all\"]`: Returns all fields, including those with `null` values
- `[\"name\", \"status\", ...]`: Returns only the specified fields

For detailed usage, examples, and field discovery, see [Response Field Control](https://developer.productboard.com/v2.0.0/reference/response-field-control).
"

merged_openapi_EntitySearchReturn.description: Controls which fields are included in the response.

fields.description:
A key-value definition fields that are part of the entity.
Key represents field id (eg. name, description, or UUID)

relationships.description:
A key-value definition of possible relationships to other entities.
Key represents the relationship type to other entities (child, parent, link)

filters.description:
Available filter parameters for this entity type.
Use these as query parameters on `GET /v2/entities` or as keys in `POST /v2/entities/search` request body.
Not all entity types support the same filters — this list is type-specific.

name.description:
The query parameter name to use when filtering.
Use this exact name as a query parameter on `GET /v2/entities`
or as a key in the `POST /v2/entities/search` request body.

field.description: The field ID this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a field.

relationship.description: The relationship key this filter applies to, when the filter is based on a relationship.

requiredScopes.description:
OAuth2 scopes required to use this filter,
beyond the standard entity scopes. Omitted when no additional scopes are needed.

merged_openapi_ConfigurationFilterParame.description:
Describes a single filter parameter available for an entity type.
The `name` is the exact query parameter name to use when filtering entities.

type.description: The value type expected for this filter.

format.description: Additional format hint for the value.

merged_openapi_ConfigurationFilterSchema.description: Describes the expected value type and format for a filter parameter.

cardinality.description: Whether the entity can have single or multiple relationships of a given type

mandatory.description:
Specifies whether the entity must have such relationship to a target entity (e.g. it must have parent).
If a relationship is mandatory and can have multiple entity types as targets, then there must exist *at least
one* relationship to an entity that is of any of the specified types.

For example, a feature must have a parent that is either a component, or a product.

merged_openapi_ConfigurationFieldLifecyc.description:
Defines the available operations for a field during different lifecycle stages.
Possible lifecycle stages are `create`, `update` and `patch`.
  - `create` defines what operations are allowed when creating an entity
  - `update` defines what operations are allowed when using a simple patch via `data.fields`
  - `patch` defines what operations are allowed when using a JSON Patch via `data.patch` array

set.description: Whether the field value can be set (assigned).

clear.description: Whether the field value can be cleared (set to null/empty).

addItems.description: Whether items can be added to the field (for list-type fields).

removeItems.description: Whether items can be removed from the field (for list-type fields).

merged_openapi_ConfigurationFieldLifecyc.description: Defines the specific operations allowed during a lifecycle stage.

data.description:

For large sets: contains preview or empty.

next.description:

merged_openapi_FieldValues.description:
Configuration for field values. Provides inline data for small sets
and/or a reference link for large/dynamic sets.

Interpretation:
- `data` only → Complete set inline
- `data` + `links.next` → Preview in data, full list at link
- `data` empty + `links.next` → Fully dynamic, fetch from link

merged_openapi_FieldValueItem.description: A single allowed value for a select-type or status field.

id.description:
The unique identifier of the field. For simple fields like "name", 
for nested fields like "timeframe.startDate", for UUID custom fields.

name.description: The display name of the field.

path.description: The path used for API operations. Uses "/fields/" prefix and nested paths for properties.

schema.description: Inline JSON Schema that defines this field's structure.

lifecycle.description:
Defines the available operations for a field during different lifecycle stages.
Possible lifecycle stages are `create`, `update` and `patch`.
  - `create` defines what operations are allowed when creating an entity
  - `update` defines what operations are allowed when using a simple patch via `data.fields`
  - `patch` defines what operations are allowed when using a JSON Patch via `data.patch` array

merged_openapi_ConfigurationField.description:

merged_openapi_EntityRelationships.description:

The list is paginated, to see the rest of the relationships follow the URL contained in the
`links.next` attribute.

merged_openapi_EntityRelationship.description:
Represents a relationship between two entities.
The source entity is implicit from the request context (the entity being queried).

merged_openapi_EntityRelationshipType.description:
"The type of the relationship to be established.

## Relationships
- `parent`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity is a child of the target
- `child`: Hierarchical relationship indicating the entity contains the target
- `link`: Generic bidirectional relationship without semantic meaning
- `isBlockedBy`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity cannot proceed until the target is resolved
- `isBlocking`: Dependency relationship indicating the entity prevents progress on the target
"

merged_openapi_EntityRelationshipCreate.description: Payload representing a new relationship towards target entity.

The exact types available may vary based on the configuration of the workspace.

merged_openapi_TimeframeFieldValue.description:
"Time period with start and end dates and granularity level for Entities.

This object contains:
- A `startDate` marking the beginning of the timeframe
- An `endDate` marking the conclusion of the timeframe
- A `granularity` indicating the time precision level (`year`, `quarter`, `month`, `day`)

## Behavior
- Both `startDate` and `endDate` use ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `endDate` must be on or after `startDate`
- `granularity` affects how dates are displayed and interpreted in the UI
- All fields are optional, allowing partial timeframe specification
- Used for planning, scheduling, time-based filtering, and defining work periods for initiatives, features, and releases
- Supports different planning granularities from daily sprints to quarterly planning

## Examples

**Monthly planning timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-01-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"month\"
}
```

**Quarterly initiative timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-03-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"quarter\"
}
```

**Annual strategic timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-01\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-12-31\",
  \"granularity\": \"year\"
}
```

**Sprint timeframe:**
```json
{
  \"startDate\": \"2024-01-15\",
  \"endDate\": \"2024-01-29\",
  \"granularity\": \"day\"
}
```
"

merged_openapi_HealthFieldValue.description:
"Health status of a Entities with tracking metadata and history.

This object contains:
- A unique `id` for the health record
- The current `status` (`notSet`, `onTrack`, `atRisk`, `offTrack`)
- The calculation `mode` (`manual` or `calculated`)
- Timestamp and user tracking information
- Optional comment providing context
- Previous status for change tracking

## Behavior
- The `status` field indicates current health state
- The `mode` field determines how health is set:
  - `manual`: Status explicitly set by users with optional comments
  - `calculated`: Status automatically derived from system logic or child entities
- `lastUpdatedAt` is automatically maintained by the system
- `previousStatus` tracks the last health state for change detection
- `createdBy` identifies who set the health status (for manual mode)
- Read-only field populated by the system after health updates
- Used to monitor work item health across the product hierarchy and track status changes over time

## Examples

**Manual health with comment:**
```json
{
  \"id\": \"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000\",
  \"status\": \"atRisk\",
  \"mode\": \"manual\",
  \"lastUpdatedAt\": \"2024-01-15T10:30:00Z\",
  \"comment\": \"Resource constraints affecting timeline\",
  \"createdBy\": {
    \"email\": \"manager@company.com\"
  },
  \"previousStatus\": \"onTrack\"
}
```

**Calculated health:**
```json
{
  \"id\": \"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001\",
  \"status\": \"onTrack\",
  \"mode\": \"calculated\",
  \"lastUpdatedAt\": \"2024-01-15T15:45:00Z\",
  \"previousStatus\": \"notSet\"
}
```
"

merged_openapi_HealthUpdateFieldValue.description:
"Represents the health status of a Entities, either manually set or automatically calculated.

This object contains:
- The mode of health tracking (`manual` or `calculated`)
- The health `status` (required only when mode is `manual`)
- An optional `comment` to provide context
- Metadata on who created the update (`createdBy`)

### Behavior
- If `mode` is `\"manual\"`, the `status` field is **required**, and you may include an optional `comment`.
- If `mode` is `\"calculated\"`, **do not provide** the `status` or `comment` fields – they will be ignored or cause validation errors.

### Use cases
- Set `manual` mode to explicitly assign a health status with reasoning.
- Use `calculated` mode when health is derived from internal logic or automation within Productboard.

### Examples

**Manual mode:**
```json
{
  \"mode\": \"manual\",
  \"status\": \"atRisk\",
  \"comment\": \"Project timeline at risk due to resource constraints\"
}
```

**Calculated mode:**
```json
{
  \"mode\": \"calculated\"
}
```
"

currentValue.description: The current progress value being tracked.

merged_openapi_ProgressFieldValue.description:
"Measurable progress tracking for key results with start, target, and current values.

This object contains:
- A `startValue` representing the baseline measurement
- A `targetValue` representing the goal to achieve
- A `currentValue` representing the present measurement

## Behavior
- All values are floating-point numbers allowing decimal precision
- Values can represent various metrics (percentages, counts, amounts, etc.)
- The `currentValue` is updated to track progress toward the `targetValue`
- Progress calculation: `(currentValue - startValue) / (targetValue - startValue) * 100`
- Supports both positive and negative progress directions
- Currently supported only for key results
- Used to track quantifiable metrics like revenue, users, and performance indicators

## Examples

**Percentage-based progress:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 0.0,
  \"targetValue\": 100.0,
  \"currentValue\": 75.5
}
```

**Revenue tracking:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 50000.0,
  \"targetValue\": 100000.0,
  \"currentValue\": 87500.0
}
```

**User count growth:**
```json
{
  \"startValue\": 1000.0,
  \"targetValue\": 5000.0,
  \"currentValue\": 3250.0
}
```
"

merged_openapi_WorkProgressFieldValue.description:
"Work progress percentage for Entities, tracking completion status from 0-100%.

This object contains:
- A numeric `value` representing completion percentage (0-100)
- A `mode` indicating how the progress is calculated (`manual`, `statusBased`, or `calculated`)

## Behavior
- The `value` field must be an integer between 0 and 100 (inclusive)
- The `mode` field determines how progress is tracked and updated:
  - `manual`: Progress is set directly by users
  - `statusBased`: Progress is automatically calculated based on entity status
  - `calculated`: Progress is derived from child entity relationships (parent/link)
- Different entity types support different modes:
  - **subfeature**: `manual`, `statusBased`
  - **feature**: `manual`, `statusBased`, `calculated`
  - **initiative**: `manual`, `calculated`
  - **objective**: `manual`, `calculated`
- When mode is `calculated`, the system automatically updates the value based on relationships
- Used to track completion percentage across the product hierarchy and monitor progress aggregation

## Examples

**Manual progress:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 75,
  \"mode\": \"manual\"
}
```

**Status-based progress:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 50,
  \"mode\": \"statusBased\"
}
```

**Calculated progress from relationships:**
```json
{
  \"value\": 85,
  \"mode\": \"calculated\"
}
```
"

value.description: The work progress percentage value between 0 and 100 (inclusive).

merged_openapi_MemberSearchData.description:
Search data that accepts both new structured format and legacy flat format.
If `filter`, `search`, or `return` is present, the new format is used. Otherwise, the legacy flat format is assumed.

merged_openapi_MemberSearchFilter.description:
Structured filters for member search. Different filter groups use AND logic.
All filters are optional.

email.description:
Filter by email address. Single string or array (OR logic), case-insensitive.
Requires `members_pii:read` scope.

role.description: Filter by role. Single string or array (OR logic).

disabled.description:
Exclusive filter for disabled members. When `true`, returns **only** disabled members.
When absent, disabled members are excluded by default.
Use `return.includeDisabled` instead if you want disabled members alongside active ones.

invitationPending.description:
Exclusive filter for members with pending invitations. When `true`, returns **only** pending members.
When absent, pending members are excluded by default.
Use `return.includeInvitationPending` instead if you want pending members alongside accepted ones.

merged_openapi_MemberSearchFilterFields.description:
Field-level filters. Multiple values within a field use OR logic.
Email matching is case-insensitive. Unknown fields return a 400 error.

query.description:
Full-text search query. Performs case-insensitive partial match on member name or email.
Requires `members_pii:read` scope.

merged_openapi_MemberSearchSearch.description: Full-text search options. Combined with filters using AND logic.

includeDisabled.description:
Include disabled members alongside active members. Default false.
If `filter.fields.disabled` is also set, the filter takes precedence.

includeInvitationPending.description:
Include members with pending invitations alongside accepted members. Default false.
If `filter.fields.invitationPending` is also set, the filter takes precedence.

merged_openapi_MemberSearchReturn.description: Controls inclusion of normally-excluded members in the response.

merged_openapi_Member.description: Represents a member (user) in the workspace.

role.description: Role of the member in the workspace.

invitationPending.description: Whether the member has a pending invitation that has not yet been accepted.

teams.description: List of teams the member belongs to. Always included in the response.

merged_openapi_NotePatch.description:
"A list of patch operations to perform on a Note field.
Each operation can be one of `set`, `clear`, `addItems`, or `removeItems`.

**Supported Operations:**
- `set`: Set a field to a specific value (supports all fields)
- `clear`: Clear/reset a field to its default value (supports owner, tags, content)
- `addItems`: Add items to array fields (supports tags, conversation content)
- `removeItems`: Remove items from array fields (supports tags, conversation content)

**Field-specific support:**
- **owner**: set, clear
- **tags**: set, clear, addItems, removeItems
- **archived**: set
- **processed**: set
- **name**: set
- **content** (textNote): set
- **content** (conversationNote): set, addItems, removeItems

**Operation Compatibility Rules (for each field):**
- Cannot combine `set`/`clear` with `addItems`/`removeItems` operations on the same field
- Cannot combine both `set` and `clear` operations on the same field
- Can combine `addItems` and `removeItems` together on the same field (for array operations)
"

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`.

path.description:
"The path to the field to be modified.
Common field paths: \"owner\", \"tags\", \"archived\", \"processed\", \"name\", \"content\"
"

merged_openapi_NotePatchOperation.description:
"This patch operation modifies a field value using set, addItems, or removeItems operations.

**Operation Types:**
- `set`: Replace the entire field value
- `addItems`: Add items to array fields (tags, conversation content)
- `removeItems`: Remove items from array fields (tags, conversation content)
"

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Must be `clear`.

path.description:
"The path to the field to be cleared.
Common field paths: \"owner\", \"tags\", \"content\"
"

merged_openapi_NotePatchOperationClear.description:
"This patch operation clears/resets the value of a field to its default state.

**Supported Fields:**
- `owner`: Clears the owner (sets to null)
- `tags`: Clears all tags (sets to empty array)
- `content`: Clears content (for conversationNote/opportunityNote notes)
"

merged_openapi_PluginIntegrationConnecti.description:
A plugin integration connection resource, representing the relationship between
a Productboard entity and an entity in the third-party system.

The `id` is the Productboard entity ID. The connection is scoped to a specific
plugin integration identified in the URL path.
Domain attributes of a plugin integration connection.

self.description: URL to retrieve this connection.

id.description: The Productboard entity ID this connection belongs to.

merged_openapi_PluginIntegrationConnecti.description: Minimal reference to a plugin integration connection, returned in mutation responses.

merged_openapi_ConnectionState.description:
The state of a plugin integration connection. Determined by the `state` property.

| State | Meaning | Push button appearance |
|---|---|---|
| `connected` | Entity linked to third-party entity | Shows `label`; click opens `targetUrl` |
| `error` | Connection attempt failed | Shows error icon with `message` |
| `progress` | Establishing asynchronously | Shows loading indicator |
| `initial` | No connection (default) | Shows integration's `initialState.label` |

label.description: Text on the push button when connected. Typically the entity's current status.

hoverLabel.description: Text on the push button on hover (temporarily replaces `label`). Typically shows the linked entity's identifier.

tooltip.description: Longer tooltip text shown on hover. Ideal for a detailed description of the linked entity.

color.description: Background color of the push button.

targetUrl.description: URL that opens when the user clicks the connected push button. Should link to the connected entity in the third-party system.

merged_openapi_ConnectedState.description:
Represents a successfully established connection. The push button displays custom
label, hover text, tooltip, color, and a link to the connected entity.

message.description: Human-readable error message explaining why the connection could not be established. Shown in the error popup in the Productboard UI.
message.example: You don't have permission to create an issue.

debugDetail.description:
Additional debugging information set by Productboard when a push fails on
Productboard's side. **Not visible in the UI** — for developer use during
integration development only. Never set by the partner.
debugDetail.example: JSON decoding error: field 'targetUrl' is missing

merged_openapi_ErrorState.description: Represents a failed connection attempt. The push button displays an error icon with the provided message.

merged_openapi_ProgressState.description:
"Represents an in-progress connection attempt. The push button shows a loading
indicator.

Use when processing is asynchronous: respond with `progress` immediately from your
action endpoint, then call Configure Connection with the final state once done.
"

merged_openapi_InitialConnectionState.description:
Represents the default, unconnected state. The push button shows the integration's
`initialState.label`. Setting a connection to `initial` via Configure Connection
is equivalent to deleting it.

merged_openapi_TeamMemberResource.description:
A team member with fields and links.
Name and email are redacted to [redacted] without the members:pii:read scope.

name.description:
The name of the team.

# Validation
- Required for creation
- Must be between 1 and 255 characters
- Must be unique per workspace

# Filtering
Supports exact match via `name` parameter and partial match via `query` parameter on GET /v2/teams.

handle.description:
Unique handle for @mentions.

# Validation
- Required for creation
- Lowercase letters and numbers only (no uppercase, spaces, or special characters)
- Must be unique per workspace

description.description:
"Optional description of the team.

# Validation
- Optional field
- Maximum length: 10000 characters
"

merged_openapi_TeamCreateOrUpdateFields.description:
Fields for creating or updating a team.
For creation, `name` and `handle` are required.
For updates, the `fields` object is required but all individual field properties within it are optional.

**Note:** `avatarUrl` is read-only and cannot be set via API. Avatar management is only available through the UI.

Each item must include either `id` (space membership UUID) or `email`.

**New format** (recommended): Uses a structured `filter` object with nested field groups.
**Legacy format** (deprecated): Uses flat properties directly under `data`.

Both formats are accepted. The legacy format will continue to work but is deprecated
in favor of the new structured format. Format is detected by the presence of the `filter` key.
"

A collection of fields that can be used to create or update a Note.
It is a subset of `NoteFields` that is used for creation or update operations.

notes_RelationshipsFieldValue.description:
Array of relationships to create with the note.
Used only for POST /v2/notes creation.

notes_NotePatch.description:
"A list of patch operations to perform on a Note field.
Each operation can be one of `set`, `clear`, `addItems`, or `removeItems`.

**Supported Operations:**
- `set`: Set a field to a specific value (supports all fields)
- `clear`: Clear/reset a field to its default value (supports owner, tags, content)
- `addItems`: Add items to array fields (supports tags, conversation content)
- `removeItems`: Remove items from array fields (supports tags, conversation content)

**Field-specific support:**
- **owner**: set, clear
- **tags**: set, clear, addItems, removeItems
- **archived**: set
- **processed**: set
- **name**: set
- **content** (textNote): set
- **content** (conversationNote): set, addItems, removeItems

**Operation Compatibility Rules (for each field):**
- Cannot combine `set`/`clear` with `addItems`/`removeItems` operations on the same field
- Cannot combine both `set` and `clear` operations on the same field
- Can combine `addItems` and `removeItems` together on the same field (for array operations)
"

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Can be one of `set`, `addItems`, `removeItems`.

path.description:
"The path to the field to be modified.
Common field paths: \"owner\", \"tags\", \"archived\", \"processed\", \"name\", \"content\"
"

notes_NotePatchOperation.description:
"This patch operation modifies a field value using set, addItems, or removeItems operations.

**Operation Types:**
- `set`: Replace the entire field value
- `addItems`: Add items to array fields (tags, conversation content)
- `removeItems`: Remove items from array fields (tags, conversation content)
"

op.description:
The operation to perform on the field.
Must be `clear`.

path.description:
"The path to the field to be cleared.
Common field paths: \"owner\", \"tags\", \"content\"
"

notes_NotePatchOperationClear.description:
"This patch operation clears/resets the value of a field to its default state.

**Supported Fields:**
- `owner`: Clears the owner (sets to null)
- `tags`: Clears all tags (sets to empty array)
- `content`: Clears content (for conversationNote/opportunityNote notes)
"

- `data` + `links.next` → Preview in `data`, full list at link
- `data` empty + `links.next` → Fully dynamic, fetch from link

This field is **write-only** and never returned in API responses.

- `Bearer <token>`

Custom headers included in every outgoing request from Productboard to your action

integrationId.description: ID of the plugin integration whose button was clicked.

trigger.description:
Identifies which button the user clicked:
- `button.push` — clicked to connect / push the entity
- `button.unlink` — clicked to unlink the connected entity
- `button.dismiss` — dismissed an error popup

html.description: URL to the entity detail page in the Productboard web application.

feature.description: The Productboard entity associated with the clicked button.

name.description: Display name of the user.

triggeredByUser.description: The Productboard user who triggered the action.

connection.description:

to update the connection state asynchronously after processing the action.

links.description: Relevant links for this action notification.

plugin_integrations_PluginIntegrationCon.description:
A plugin integration connection resource, representing the relationship between
a Productboard entity and an entity in the third-party system.

The `id` is the Productboard entity ID. The connection is scoped to a specific
plugin integration identified in the URL path.
Domain attributes of a plugin integration connection.

self.description: URL to retrieve this connection.

id.description: The Productboard entity ID this connection belongs to.

plugin_integrations_PluginIntegrationCon.description: Minimal reference to a plugin integration connection, returned in mutation responses.

plugin_integrations_ConnectionState.description:
The state of a plugin integration connection. Determined by the `state` property.

| State | Meaning | Push button appearance |
|---|---|---|
| `connected` | Entity linked to third-party entity | Shows `label`; click opens `targetUrl` |
| `error` | Connection attempt failed | Shows error icon with `message` |
| `progress` | Establishing asynchronously | Shows loading indicator |
| `initial` | No connection (default) | Shows integration's `initialState.label` |

label.description: Text on the push button when connected. Typically the entity's current status.

hoverLabel.description: Text on the push button on hover (temporarily replaces `label`). Typically shows the linked entity's identifier.

tooltip.description: Longer tooltip text shown on hover. Ideal for a detailed description of the linked entity.

color.description: Background color of the push button.

targetUrl.description: URL that opens when the user clicks the connected push button. Should link to the connected entity in the third-party system.

plugin_integrations_ConnectedState.description:
Represents a successfully established connection. The push button displays custom
label, hover text, tooltip, color, and a link to the connected entity.

message.description: Human-readable error message explaining why the connection could not be established. Shown in the error popup in the Productboard UI.
message.example: You don't have permission to create an issue.

debugDetail.description:
Additional debugging information set by Productboard when a push fails on
Productboard's side. **Not visible in the UI** — for developer use during
integration development only. Never set by the partner.
debugDetail.example: JSON decoding error: field 'targetUrl' is missing

plugin_integrations_ErrorState.description: Represents a failed connection attempt. The push button displays an error icon with the provided message.

plugin_integrations_ProgressState.description:
"Represents an in-progress connection attempt. The push button shows a loading
indicator.

Use when processing is asynchronous: respond with `progress` immediately from your
action endpoint, then call Configure Connection with the final state once done.
"

plugin_integrations_InitialConnectionSta.description:
Represents the default, unconnected state. The push button shows the integration's
`initialState.label`. Setting a connection to `initial` via Configure Connection
is equivalent to deleting it.

handle.description: Unique handle for @mentions. Lowercase alphanumeric only.

avatarUrl.description:
URL of the team's avatar image. Read-only.
Returns `null` if no avatar is set.

teams_TeamMemberResource.description:
A team member with fields and links.
Name and email are redacted to [redacted] without the members:pii:read scope.

name.description:
The name of the team.

# Validation
- Required for creation
- Must be between 1 and 255 characters
- Must be unique per workspace

# Filtering
Supports exact match via `name` parameter and partial match via `query` parameter on GET /v2/teams.

handle.description:
Unique handle for @mentions.

# Validation
- Required for creation
- Lowercase letters and numbers only (no uppercase, spaces, or special characters)
- Must be unique per workspace

description.description:
"Optional description of the team.

# Validation
- Optional field
- Maximum length: 10000 characters
"

teams_TeamCreateOrUpdateFields.description:
Fields for creating or updating a team.
For creation, `name` and `handle` are required.
For updates, the `fields` object is required but all individual field properties within it are optional.

**Note:** `avatarUrl` is read-only and cannot be set via API. Avatar management is only available through the UI.

path.description: The field to operate on.

value.description:
Member references for the operation.
Each item must include either `id` (space membership UUID) or `email`.

oneOf.description: Operation with a value (addItems, removeItems, set)

op.description: Clear all items from the field.

path.description: The field to clear.

teams_TeamPatchOperation.description: A patch operation for managing list-type fields on a team.

teams_TeamSearchRequest.description: Request body for searching teams with filters.

ids.description: (Legacy) Filter by team UUIDs (OR logic). Use `filter.id` instead.

names.description: (Legacy) Filter by team names, case-insensitive (OR logic). Use `filter.fields.name` instead.

handles.description: (Legacy) Filter by team handles, case-insensitive (OR logic). Use `filter.fields.handle` instead.

teams_TeamSearchData.description:
Search data that accepts both new structured format and legacy flat format.
If `filter` or `search` is present, the new format is used. Otherwise, the legacy flat format is assumed.

teams_TeamSearchFilter.description:
Structured filters for team search. Different filter groups use AND logic.
All filters are optional.

name.description: Filter by team name. Single string or array (OR logic), case-insensitive.

handle.description: Filter by team handle. Single string or array (OR logic), case-insensitive.

teams_TeamSearchFilterFields.description:
Field-level filters. Multiple values within a field use OR logic.
String matching is case-insensitive.

query.description: Full-text search query. Performs case-insensitive partial match on team name and handle.

teams_TeamSearchSearch.description: Full-text search options. Combined with filters using AND logic.

pointer.description: JSON Pointer [RFC6901] to the associated entity in the request document.

parameter.description:
Query parameter causing the error.
Used when the error is related to a query parameter rather than request body.

C_teams_ErrorSource.description: An object containing references to the source of the error. It helps with location of the problematic field or parameter.

code.description:
"A unique, machine-readable and stable code that identifies this error.
Must be consistent across all services and provide clear categorization.
Format: `category.subcategory.specificError`
"

title.description:
A short, human-readable summary of the problem that doesn't change
from occurrence to occurrence of the problem. Should provide immediate
understanding of the error type.

detail.description:
A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
May include suggestions for resolution but should not expose sensitive information.

C_teams_Error.description: Individual error object containing the details and context.

id.description:
A unique identifier for this specific occurrence of the problem.
It carries unique request/response ID which allows client
and server to reference the exact instance of the error
in logs or support communication.

errors.description: Array of error objects, multiple errors can be returned.

C_teams_ObfuscatedValue.description: The value `[redacted]`. Used to hide personally identifiable information in cases the request doesn't have the required `members:pii:read` OAuth2 scope.

webhooks_WebhookType.description: Resource type identifier for webhook subscriptions.

id.description: Unique identifier of the created webhook subscription.

self.description: URL to fetch the full webhook subscription.

webhooks_WebhookReference.description: Lightweight reference returned after creating a webhook subscription.

webhooks_WebhookReferenceResponse.description: Response envelope for a newly created webhook subscription.

id.description: Unique identifier for this webhook subscription.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the subscription was created.

webhooks_WebhookResponse.description: Response envelope for a single webhook subscription.

webhooks_WebhookListResponse.description: Response envelope for a paginated list of webhook subscriptions.

name.description: Human-readable label for this subscription.

events.description:
Event types to subscribe to. At least one required; no duplicates allowed.
The full list of supported values is defined in the `WebhookEventType` schema.

webhooks_WebhookSubscriptionFields.description: Domain attributes for creating a webhook subscription.

name.description: Human-readable label for this subscription.

events.description: Event types that trigger this subscription.

webhooks_WebhookSubscriptionResponseFiel.description:
"Domain attributes returned in webhook subscription responses.
Note: `notification.headers.authorization` is intentionally absent from responses to protect secrets.
"

webhooks_WebhookCreateRequestBody.description: Request body for creating a webhook subscription.

self.description: URL to retrieve this webhook subscription.

webhooks_WebhookSubscriptionLinks.description: Links for this webhook subscription resource.

webhooks_WebhookEventType.description: Identifies the type of entity change that triggers a webhook notification.

url.description:
HTTPS endpoint to receive notifications. Requirements:
- Must use `https` (encrypted)
- Must be publicly reachable (no `localhost`, private IPs, or internal Kubernetes addresses)

webhooks_WebhookNotificationConfig.description: Where and how Productboard sends notifications (used in create requests).

webhooks_WebhookNotificationConfigRespon.description: Notification configuration as returned in responses (secret omitted).

webhooks_WebhookPayloadVersion.description: Version of the notification payload structure. Currently only `1` is supported.

authorization.description:
Raw value sent in the `Authorization` header. Supports any scheme:
- `Basic aGVsbG86d29ybGQ=`
- `Bearer <token>`
- Any custom value

webhooks_WebhookNotificationHeaders.description:
Custom headers included in every outgoing request from Productboard to your endpoint
(notifications). Use this to authenticate incoming requests.

id.description:
Entity ID that changed. Absent for `hierarchy-entity.custom-field-value.updated`
and `feature-release-assignment.updated`.

updatedAttributes.description:
Names of the API fields that changed. Only present on `*.updated` events.
Uses public API field names (e.g., `status`, `name`, `parent`).

webhooks_WebhookNotificationPayloadV1.description: Notification payload delivered to the subscriber endpoint.

webhooks_WebhookNotificationRequestBody.description: Request body delivered to the subscriber endpoint on each entity change event.

target.description: API URL of the entity whose change triggered this notification.

webhooks_LinksTarget.description: Standard V1 link — points to the changed entity.

webhooks_LinksFeatureNote.description: V1 link shape for insight events — references both feature and note.

value.description:
Numeric values for quantitative data in Entities.

## Behavior
- Accepts both integers and floating-point numbers
- Supports positive, negative, and zero values
- No inherent minimum or maximum constraints (field-specific limits may apply)
- Used for effort estimation, scores, ratings, budgets, and metrics

"HTML-formatted text content for Entities.

## Behavior
- Accepts HTML-formatted strings up to 524KB (524,288 characters)
- Can be empty string (minLength: 0)
- Supports rich formatting including bold, italic, links, lists
  - Follow [Rich Text Formatting guidelines](https://developer.productboard.com/reference/rich-text-formatting)
- Used for detailed descriptions, specifications, documentation, and formatted comments
"

"Short text content for Entities with a 255 character limit.

## Behavior
- Accepts any string content up to 255 characters
- Can be empty string (minLength: 0)
- Supports UTF-8 encoding for international characters
- Used for short descriptions, labels, identifiers, and status messages
"

Valid URL for Entities.

## Behavior
- Must be a valid URL following URI format standards
- Supports various protocols (http, https, ftp, etc.)
- Validates for proper URL structure and format
- Used for external links, documentation references, and resource URLs

Universally unique identifier (UUID) for Entities.

## Behavior
- Validates for proper UUID structure and format
- Used for entity IDs and references to other entities
- Case-insensitive but typically lowercase

errors.title: Request malformed
errors.detail: The request body contains invalid JSON syntax. Expected ',' or '}' at line 5, column 12.

errors.title: Request malformed
errors.detail: The Content-Type header must be 'application/json'. Received 'text/plain'.

_webhooks_UnauthorizedResponse.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication credentials

errors.title: Invalid authentication
errors.detail: Authentication credentials are missing.

errors.title: Invalid authentication
errors.detail: The provided authentication credentials are invalid or have expired. Please obtain new credentials and try again.

errors.title: Invalid authentication
errors.detail: The authentication credentials have been revoked. Please contact your workspace administrator.

errors.title: Access denied
errors.detail: You do not have permission to perform this operation on the specified resource. Contact your workspace administrator to request access.

errors.title: Access denied
errors.detail: Your authentication token lacks the required OAuth scopes to perform this operation. Please re-authenticate with the necessary scopes.

errors.title: Access denied
errors.detail: Your user role does not permit this operation. Please contact your workspace administrator.

_webhooks_RequestTimeoutResponse.description: Request Timeout - The server did not receive a complete request within the allowed time

errors.title: Request timeout
errors.detail: The request took too long to process and was terminated. Try simplifying your request or try again later.

_webhooks_TooManyRequestsResponse.description: Too Many Requests - API rate limit exceeded, reduce request frequency and retry after the indicated time

errors.title: Rate limit exceeded
errors.detail: You have exceeded the allowed number of API requests. Please wait before making additional requests.

_webhooks_InternalServerErrorResponse.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred on the server, please retry or contact support

errors.title: Internal server error
errors.detail: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request. Please try again later. If the problem persists, contact support with the correlation ID.

_webhooks_UnprocessableEntityResponse.description: Unprocessable Entity - Validation failed (e.g., missing required fields, unknown fields)

errors.title: Validation failed
errors.detail: One or more fields failed validation.

errors.title: Validation failed
errors.detail: The field 'name' cannot be empty.

errors.title: Validation failed
errors.detail: The field 'type' has an unsupported value.

errors.title: Referenced resource not found
errors.detail: The referenced resource could not be found or you do not have access to it.

_webhooks_NotFoundResponse.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist or is not accessible

errors.title: Resource not found
errors.detail: The requested resource could not be found or you do not have permission to access it.

errors.title: Route not found
errors.detail: The requested URL path does not match any defined API route. Please check the API documentation for valid endpoints.

Type.name: type
Type.description:
The type of the entity. Currently supported PM entity types:
  - product
  - component
  - feature
  - subfeature
  - initiative
  - objective
  - keyResult
  - release
  - releaseGroup
  - user
  - company

The exact types available may vary based on the configuration of the workspace.

Fields.name: fields[]
Fields.description:
"Controls which fields are returned in the response to optimize bandwidth and tailor data to your needs.

**Options:**
- Not specified: Returns only fields with non-empty values (default)
- `fields[]=all`: Returns all fields, including those with `null` values
- `fields[]=name&fields[]=status`: Returns only the specified fields

For detailed usage, examples, and field discovery, see [Response Field Control](https://developer.productboard.com/v2.0.0/reference/response-field-control).
"

fields.name: fields
fields.description:
"Controls which fields are returned in the response to optimize bandwidth and tailor data to your needs.

**Options:**
- Not specified: Returns only fields with non-empty values (default)
- `fields[]=all`: Returns all fields, including those with `null` values
- `fields[]=name&fields[]=tags`: Returns only the specified fields

**Notes:**
- When specific fields are requested, they are returned even if they have null/empty values
- Unknown field IDs result in a 400 Bad Request error
- Combining `all` with specific field IDs results in a 400 Bad Request error

For detailed usage, examples, and field discovery, see [Response Field Control](https://developer.productboard.com/v2.0.0/reference/response-field-control).
"

"HTML-formatted text content for Entities.

## Behavior
- Accepts HTML-formatted strings up to 524KB (524,288 characters)
- Can be empty string (minLength: 0)
- Supports rich formatting including bold, italic, links, lists
  - Follow [Rich Text Formatting guidelines](https://developer.productboard.com/reference/rich-text-formatting)
- Used for detailed descriptions, specifications, documentation, and formatted comments
"
